1741	Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
1742	
1743	== Axial Format Terms
1744	
1745	{axf_format_s}
1746	specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
1747	{axrg_assignment_s}
1748	use a particular
1749	{axf_format}
1750	to encode
1751	{axrg_variable}
1752	values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
1753	
1754	[[axf_format]]
1755	{axf_format}::
1756	A
1757	{axl_motif}
1758	representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
1759	{axf_format_s}
1760	determine how
1761	{axrg_assignment_s}
1762	are written to and read from files.
1763	
1764	[[axf_bash]]
1765	{axf_bash}::
1766	A
1767	{axl_motif}
1768	representing bash script format for configuration.
1769	Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
1770	Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
1771	
1772	[[axf_makefile]]
1773	{axf_makefile}::
1774	A
1775	{axl_motif}
1776	representing makefile format for configuration.
1777	Files are includable via `include file`.
1778	Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
1779	
1780	[[axf_json]]
1781	{axf_json}::
1782	A
1783	{axl_motif}
1784	representing JSON format for configuration.
1785	Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
1786	Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
1787	
1788	[[axf_yaml]]
1789	{axf_yaml}::
1790	A
1791	{axl_motif}
1792	representing YAML format for configuration.
1793	Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
1794	Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
1795	
1796	== Axial Regime Terms
1797	
1798	{axrg_regime_s}
1799	are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
1800	
1801	[[axrg_regime]]
1802	{axrg_regime}::
1803	A
1804	{axl_motif}
1805	representing a structured configuration system.
1806	A
1807	{axrg_regime}
1808	binds related
1809	{axrg_variable_s}
1810	under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
1811	The specification defines what can be configured;
1812	{axrg_assignment_s}
1813	provide specific values.
1814	
1815	[[axrg_variable]]
1816	{axrg_variable}::
1817	A
1818	{axl_motif}
1819	representing a named configuration element at schema level.
1820	Each
1821	{axrg_variable}
1822	belongs to an
1823	{axrg_regime}
1824	and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
1825	{axrg_variable_s}
1826	are the "what can be configured" definitions;
1827	{axrg_assignment_s}
1828	bind values to them.
1829	
1830	[[axrg_assignment]]
1831	{axrg_assignment}::
1832	A
1833	{axl_motif}
1834	representing a specific value bound to an
1835	{axrg_variable}.
1836	A single binding — one variable, one value.
1837	{axrg_assignment_s}
1838	are conveyed by an
1839	{axrg_carrier},
1840	whose concrete form and
1841	{axf_format}
1842	depend on the regime's provenance.
1843	
1844	[[axrg_carrier]]
1845	{axrg_carrier}::
1846	A
1847	{axl_motif}
1848	representing the artifact holding one instance's complete set of
1849	{axrg_assignment_s}
1850	for a single
1851	{axrg_regime}.
1852	The carrier's concrete form is selected by its provenance
1853	{axd_dimension}:
1854	{axrd_file_sourced}
1855	manifests as a file at a path (encoding via
1856	{axf_format});
1857	{axrd_constructed}
1858	as values assembled by a defining module and sourced into consumers;
1859	{axrd_env_inherited}
1860	as an inherited process environment scope.
1861	A
1862	{axrd_singleton}
1863	regime has exactly one carrier;
1864	a
1865	{axrd_manifold}
1866	regime has one carrier per instance.
1867	Only a
1868	{axrd_file_sourced}
1869	carrier has a path locus: the singleton case resolves to a single path, the manifold case to one templated path per instance.
1870	Whether that locus earns a dedicated quoin or is composed in prose from a root quoin plus a literal leaf is a per-specification judgment, not a property of the motif.
1871	Virtual carriers
1872	({axrd_constructed},
1873	{axrd_env_inherited})
1874	have no consumer-facing path.
1875	
1876	[[axrg_prefix]]
1877	{axrg_prefix}::
1878	A
1879	{axl_motif}
1880	representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
1881	{axrg_variable_s}
1882	within an
1883	{axrg_regime}.
1884	Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
1885	Once established, an
1886	{axrg_prefix}
1887	becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
1888	Changing an
1889	{axrg_prefix}
1890	requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
1891	
1892	== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions
1893	
1894	Provenance dimensions qualify an
1895	{axrg_carrier}
1896	to declare its concrete form and how its
1897	{axrg_assignment_s}
1898	arrive at
1899	{axvr_kindle}
1900	time.
1901	They are declared on
1902	{axvr_regime}
1903	to state which provenance(s) a regime's carrier supports.
1904	
1905	[[axrd_file_sourced]]
1906	{axrd_file_sourced}::
1907	Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
1908	The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.
1909	
1910	[[axrd_env_inherited]]
1911	{axrd_env_inherited}::
1912	Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
1913	The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.
1914	
1915	[[axrd_constructed]]
1916	{axrd_constructed}::
1917	Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
1918	Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
1919	
1920	== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
1921	
1922	Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
1923	Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
1924	
1925	=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
1926	
1927	{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
1928	The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
1929	
1930	[source,asciidoc]
1931	----
1932	[[rbrv_sigil]]
1933	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
1934	{rbrv_sigil}::
1935	Vessel identifier for build configurations.
1936	----
1937	
1938	The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
1939	The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
1940	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1941	in the definition text that follows.
1942	
1943	[[axvr_regime]]
1944	{axvr_regime}::
1945	Voices a regime definition.
1946	The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
1947	{axrg_variable_s}
1948	under a namespace prefix.
1949	+
1950	{axd_dimension_s}:
1951	{axf_format}
1952	(specifying how
1953	{axrg_assignment_s}
1954	are carried).
1955	
1956	[[axvr_variable]]
1957	{axvr_variable}::
1958	Voices a regime variable definition.
1959	The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
1960	The definition text must include, as its second
1961	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1962	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1963	{axrg_regime}).
1964	This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
1965	+
1966	{axd_dimension_s}:
1967	{axd_required}
1968	or
1969	{axd_optional},
1970	type
1971	{axl_motif}
1972	(base type or type voicing).
1973	
1974	[[axvr_group]]
1975	{axvr_group}::
1976	Voices a regime group definition.
1977	The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
1978	{axrg_variable_s}
1979	within a regime.
1980	The definition text must include, as its second
1981	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1982	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1983	{axrg_regime}).
1984	+
1985	{axd_dimension_s}:
1986	{axd_conditional}
1987	(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
1988	
1989	[[axvr_kindle]]
1990	{axvr_kindle}::
1991	Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
1992	The annotated term is a dedicated
1993	{mcm_quoin}
1994	naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
1995	{axrg_regime}:
1996	setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
1997	
1998	[[axvr_validate]]
1999	{axvr_validate}::
2000	Voices a regime validate operation definition.
2001	The annotated term is a dedicated
2002	{mcm_quoin}
2003	naming the strict verification phase for exactly one
2004	{axrg_regime}:
2005	checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
2006	
2007	[[axvr_render]]
2008	{axvr_render}::
2009	Voices a regime render operation definition.
2010	The annotated term is a dedicated
2011	{mcm_quoin}
2012	naming the diagnostic display phase for exactly one
2013	{axrg_regime}:
2014	producing human-readable presentation of current state.
2015	Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
2016	
2017	[[axvr_list]]
2018	{axvr_list}::
2019	Voices a regime list operation definition.
2020	The annotated term is a dedicated
2021	{mcm_quoin}
2022	naming the enumeration operation for exactly one
2023	{axrd_manifold}
2024	{axrg_regime}:
2025	listing available instances.
2026	
2027	[[axvr_survey]]
2028	{axvr_survey}::
2029	Voices a regime survey operation definition.
2030	The annotated term is a dedicated
2031	{mcm_quoin}
2032	naming the read-only cross-instance summary operation for exactly one
2033	{axrd_manifold}
2034	{axrg_regime}.
2035	
2036	[[axvr_audit]]
2037	{axvr_audit}::
2038	Voices a regime audit operation definition.
2039	The annotated term is a dedicated
2040	{mcm_quoin}
2041	naming the cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for exactly one
2042	{axrd_manifold}
2043	{axrg_regime}.
2044	
2045	=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
2046	
2047	Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
2048	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
2049	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2050	from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
2051	
2052	The surface syntax:
2053	
2054	[source,asciidoc]
2055	----
2056	//axhrb_regime
2057	{rbrv_regime}
2058	
2059	//axhrgv_variable
2060	{rbrv_sigil}
2061	----
2062	
2063	The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
2064	Following text contains the
2065	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2066	that the marker reads ahead.
2067	
2068	==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2069	
2070	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2071	|===
2072	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2073	
2074	|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
2075	|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
2076	|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
2077	|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
2078	|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
2079	|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
2080	|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
2081	|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
2082	|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
2083	|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
2084	|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
2085	|===
2086	
2087	[[axhrb_regime]]
2088	{axhrb_regime}::
2089	Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
2090	Lookahead: 1
2091	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2092	— the regime this subdoc describes.
2093	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2094	{axvr_regime}
2095	in the parent document.
2096	
2097	[[axhrv_variable]]
2098	{axhrv_variable}::
2099	Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
2100	Lookahead: 1
2101	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2102	— the variable being detailed.
2103	
2104	[[axhrgb_group]]
2105	{axhrgb_group}::
2106	Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
2107	Lookahead: 1
2108	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2109	— the group being detailed.
2110	The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
2111	{axvr_group}
2112	in the parent document.
2113	
2114	[[axhrgc_gate]]
2115	{axhrgc_gate}::
2116	Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
2117	Lookahead: 2
2118	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2119	— the enumerated variable (voicing
2120	{axt_enumeration})
2121	and the specific
2122	{axt_enum_value}
2123	that activates this gate.
2124	The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
2125	
2126	[[axhrgv_variable]]
2127	{axhrgv_variable}::
2128	Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
2129	Lookahead: 1
2130	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2131	— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
2132	
2133	[[axhro_kindle]]
2134	{axhro_kindle}::
2135	Marks a regime kindle operation description in a subdocument.
2136	Lookahead: 1
2137	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2138	— the kit-level kindle operation term (which must be voiced
2139	{axvr_kindle}
2140	in the parent document).
2141	
2142	[[axhro_validate]]
2143	{axhro_validate}::
2144	Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
2145	Lookahead: 1
2146	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2147	— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
2148	{axvr_validate}
2149	in the parent document).
2150	
2151	[[axhro_render]]
2152	{axhro_render}::
2153	Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
2154	Lookahead: 1
2155	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2156	— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
2157	{axvr_render}
2158	in the parent document).
2159	
2160	[[axhro_list]]
2161	{axhro_list}::
2162	Marks a regime list operation description in a subdocument.
2163	Lookahead: 1
2164	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2165	— the kit-level list operation term (which must be voiced
2166	{axvr_list}
2167	in the parent document).
2168	Only valid on
2169	{axrd_manifold}
2170	regimes.
2171	
2172	[[axhro_survey]]
2173	{axhro_survey}::
2174	Marks a regime survey operation description in a subdocument.
2175	Lookahead: 1
2176	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2177	— the kit-level survey operation term (which must be voiced
2178	{axvr_survey}
2179	in the parent document).
2180	Only valid on
2181	{axrd_manifold}
2182	regimes.
2183	
2184	[[axhro_audit]]
2185	{axhro_audit}::
2186	Marks a regime audit operation description in a subdocument.
2187	Lookahead: 1
2188	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2189	— the kit-level audit operation term (which must be voiced
2190	{axvr_audit}
2191	in the parent document).
2192	Only valid on
2193	{axrd_manifold}
2194	regimes.
2195	
2196	==== Nesting Rules
2197	
2198	Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2199	
2200	* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
2201	* {axhrgb_group},
2202	{axhrgc_gate},
2203	and
2204	{axhrgv_variable}
2205	must appear within
2206	{axhrb_regime}
2207	scope
2208	* {axhrgc_gate}
2209	and
2210	{axhrgv_variable}
2211	must appear within
2212	{axhrgb_group}
2213	scope
2214	* Multiple
2215	{axhrgc_gate}
2216	within one group have AND semantics
2217	* {axhro_kindle},
2218	{axhro_validate},
2219	{axhro_render},
2220	{axhro_list},
2221	{axhro_survey},
2222	and
2223	{axhro_audit}
2224	must appear within
2225	{axhrb_regime}
2226	scope but outside any
2227	{axhrgb_group}
2228	scope
2229	
2230	==== Cross-Document Validation
2231	
2232	* {axhrb_regime}
2233	attribute reference must match a regime defined with
2234	{axvr_regime}
2235	in parent
2236	* {axhrgb_group}
2237	attribute reference must match a group defined with
2238	{axvr_group}
2239	under that regime
2240	* {axhrgc_gate}
2241	first attribute reference must voice
2242	{axt_enumeration};
2243	second must be an
2244	{axt_enum_value}
2245	of that specific enumeration type
2246	
2247	== Axial Lexicon Structural Terms
2248	
2249	[[axl_definition_site]]
2250	{axl_definition_site}::
2251	The location in a parent document where a linked term is defined: anchor, voicing annotation, and definition text.
2252	The
2253	{axl_definition_site}
2254	establishes the term's identity, role, and architectural relationships.
2255	Voicing annotations at the
2256	{axl_definition_site}
2257	may impose positional requirements on linked terms in the definition text that follows.
2258	
2259	[[axl_detail_site]]
2260	{axl_detail_site}::
2261	The location where a linked term's details are elaborated through hierarchy markers and prose.
2262	Typically a subdocument included from the parent, but may be inline within the parent document for compact definitions.
2263	The
2264	{axl_detail_site}
2265	is opened by a hierarchy block marker (e.g.,
2266	{axhob_operation},
2267	{axhrb_regime})
2268	and contains subordinate markers for parameters, steps, variables, or other structural elements.
2269	
2270	== Axial Voicing Operation Terms
2271	
2272	Two annotation families enable operation specification across
2273	{axl_definition_site_s}
2274	and
2275	{axl_detail_site_s},
2276	paralleling the regime annotation pattern.
2277	
2278	=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvo_*)
2279	
2280	{axvo_*} annotations appear at the
2281	{axl_definition_site}
2282	between anchor and definition, declaring the operation role of the term being defined.
2283	
2284	[[axvo_procedure]]
2285	{axvo_procedure}::
2286	Voices an
2287	{axo_procedure}
2288	definition.
2289	The annotated term defines an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
2290	No entity affiliation required.
2291	The first
2292	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2293	is the procedure being defined.
2294	+
2295	{axd_dimension_s}:
2296	exactly one lifecycle
2297	({axd_transient},
2298	{axd_longrunning},
2299	or
2300	{axd_periodic}).
2301	Optional
2302	{axd_attended}.
2303	Optional
2304	{axd_internal}.
2305	Optional
2306	{axd_grouped}
2307	(the second
2308	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2309	must be a term voiced
2310	{axvo_group}).
2311	
2312	[[axvo_method]]
2313	{axvo_method}::
2314	Voices an
2315	{axo_method}
2316	definition.
2317	The annotated term defines an executable operation belonging to an entity's interface.
2318	The first
2319	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2320	is the method being defined.
2321	The second
2322	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2323	must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
2324	{axo_entity}).
2325	+
2326	{axd_dimension_s}:
2327	exactly one lifecycle
2328	({axd_transient},
2329	{axd_longrunning},
2330	or
2331	{axd_periodic}).
2332	Optional
2333	{axd_attended}.
2334	Optional
2335	{axd_internal}.
2336	Optional
2337	{axd_grouped}
2338	(the third
2339	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2340	must be a term voiced
2341	{axvo_group}).
2342	
2343	[[axvo_group]]
2344	{axvo_group}::
2345	Voices an
2346	{axo_group}
2347	definition.
2348	The annotated term defines a named collection of related operations that share an affiliated entity.
2349	The first
2350	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2351	is the group being defined.
2352	The second
2353	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2354	must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
2355	{axo_entity}).
2356	+
2357	{axd_dimension_s}:
2358	optional
2359	{axd_tabtarget},
2360	{axd_slash_command},
2361	or other exposure mechanism specifying the default interface for external members.
2362	
2363	[[axvo_tabtarget]]
2364	{axvo_tabtarget}::
2365	Voices a tabtarget — one concrete CLI invocation routed via filename
2366	from a tabtarget-style dispatch convention.
2367	Each tabtarget receives exactly one
2368	{mcm_quoin};
2369	per-platform or per-target variants are catalogued separately rather
2370	than collapsed under a shared sprue list.
2371	+
2372	1-arity: reads one
2373	{mcm_sprue}
2374	from following text — the literal routing identifier of the concrete
2375	CLI invocation as it appears on disk.
2376	The
2377	{mcm_sprue}
2378	is the exact wire-name; the
2379	{mcm_quoin}
2380	is the concrete CLI invocation concept.
2381	Sprue-lookahead mechanism mirrors
2382	{axr_member}.
2383	+
2384	Qualified by
2385	{axd_dimension_s}
2386	in
2387	{axl_annotation}.
2388	The
2389	{axd_imprint}
2390	dimension types a filename-embedded folio slot when present;
2391	absence declares the tabtarget has no embedded folio.
2392	+
2393	Operation detail sites may attach to
2394	{axvo_tabtarget}
2395	quoins via
2396	{axhob_operation},
2397	reusing the procedure/method hierarchy markers without alteration.
2398	Second-order constraints — which markers apply, which don't,
2399	under what conditions — are deferred to future articulation.
2400	
2401	=== New Operation Dimension Terms
2402	
2403	[[axd_attended]]
2404	{axd_attended}::
2405	A
2406	{axd_dimension}
2407	indicating that a procedure or method requires human interaction during execution.
2408	Absence means unattended (fully automated).
2409	Valid on
2410	{axvo_procedure}
2411	and
2412	{axvo_method}.
2413	
2414	[[axd_internal]]
2415	{axd_internal}::
2416	A
2417	{axd_dimension}
2418	indicating that a procedure or method is an implementation building block, not a consumer-facing interface.
2419	Absence means external (published interface).
2420	Valid on
2421	{axvo_procedure}
2422	and
2423	{axvo_method}.
2424	
2425	[[axd_grouped]]
2426	{axd_grouped}::
2427	A
2428	{axd_dimension}
2429	indicating that a procedure or method belongs to a named operation group.
2430	Imposes a positional requirement on the definition text: the next
2431	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2432	after any prior positional requirements must be an
2433	{axo_group}.
2434	For
2435	{axvo_procedure}:
2436	the second linked term must be the group.
2437	For
2438	{axvo_method}:
2439	the third linked term must be the group (second is the entity).
2440	Valid on
2441	{axvo_procedure}
2442	and
2443	{axvo_method}.
2444	
2445	[[axd_tabtarget]]
2446	{axd_tabtarget}::
2447	A
2448	{axd_dimension}
2449	indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as tabtargets.
2450	Valid on
2451	{axvo_group}.
2452	
2453	[[axd_slash_command]]
2454	{axd_slash_command}::
2455	A
2456	{axd_dimension}
2457	indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as
2458	{axi_cc_slash_command_s}.
2459	Valid on
2460	{axvo_group}.
2461	
2462	[[axd_imprint]]
2463	{axd_imprint}::
2464	A
2465	{axd_dimension}
2466	indicating that a tabtarget's filename embeds a folio — a parameter
2467	value carried by the tabtarget's wire-name rather than passed as a
2468	runtime argument.
2469	1-arity: reads one
2470	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2471	from following text — the type anchor of whatever the imprint slot
2472	names.
2473	Must resolve to an existing type quoin; zero new terms minted.
2474	Valid on
2475	{axvo_tabtarget}.
2476	+
2477	Lookahead composition rule: dimensions on the annotation line consume
2478	their lookaheads in left-to-right order, after the primary voicing has
2479	consumed its own.
2480	For
2481	{axvo_tabtarget}
2482	this means the
2483	{mcm_sprue}
2484	is read first, then any
2485	{axd_imprint}
2486	attribute-reference lookahead.
2487	The rule is forward-looking — composition of multiple lookahead-bearing
2488	dimensions is not yet exercised, but future additions follow the same
2489	convention.
2490	
2491	[[axd_fact]]
2492	{axd_fact}::
2493	A
2494	{axd_dimension}
2495	indicating that an operation output is delivered as a single atomic value in a well-known named file.
2496	The file is written to the dispatch output directory; consumers read the value verbatim.
2497	The constant name (carried by the marker's existing lookahead) is the contract between producer and consumer.
2498	Valid on
2499	{axhoot_typed_output}.
2500	At the definition site, the corresponding term is voiced
2501	{axpof_fact}.
2502	
2503	[[axd_tally]]
2504	{axd_tally}::
2505	A
2506	{axd_dimension}
2507	indicating that an operation output is a fact map — a dynamically-named set of fact files sharing a common infix.
2508	The infix constant is the contract; surrounding filename segments are runtime-composed keys.
2509	Consumers check file presence or glob by infix rather than reading a single well-known filename.
2510	Valid on
2511	{axhoot_typed_output}.
2512	At the definition site, the corresponding term is voiced
2513	{axpot_tally}.
2514	
2515	[[axd_algebraic]]
2516	{axd_algebraic}::
2517	A
2518	{axd_dimension}
2519	indicating seed-derived positional encoding for an
2520	{axt_insignia}.
2521	Characters drawn from a fixed alphabet, fixed digit count, monotonic seed incremented under lock.
2522	Digit count disambiguates type within the algebraic family.
2523	Hierarchical embedding via prefix substring: a child identity's leading characters are the parent identity's value.
2524	
2525	[[axd_temporal]]
2526	{axd_temporal}::
2527	A
2528	{axd_dimension}
2529	indicating time-derived encoding for an
2530	{axt_insignia}.
2531	Datestamp component plus autonumber (e.g., YYMMDD-NNNN), allocated by filesystem enumeration.
2532	No hierarchical embedding.
2533	
2534	=== Product-of-Operation Terms
2535	
2536	[[axpof_fact]]
2537	{axpof_fact}::
2538	Voices a term as a fact file constant — a named output file carrying a single atomic value.
2539	The term's anchor names the file; consumers read the value verbatim without parsing.
2540	At detail sites, referenced by
2541	{axhoot_typed_output}
2542	with
2543	{axd_fact}.
2544	
2545	[[axpot_tally]]
2546	{axpot_tally}::
2547	Voices a term as a fact map infix — a naming template that produces dynamically-named fact files at runtime.
2548	The term's anchor names the infix constant; the full filename is composed as `«prefix»«INFIX»«suffix»` where prefix and suffix are runtime values.
2549	One file per discovered item; consumers check presence via `test -f` on composed filenames.
2550	At detail sites, referenced by
2551	{axhoot_typed_output}
2552	with
2553	{axd_tally}.
2554	
2555	=== New Operation Terms
2556	
2557	[[axo_group]]
2558	{axo_group}::
2559	A
2560	{axl_motif}
2561	representing a named collection of related
2562	{axo_procedure_s}
2563	or
2564	{axo_method_s}
2565	that share an affiliated
2566	{axo_entity}.
2567	The group provides a formal identity at the
2568	{axl_definition_site}
2569	for operation clustering that would otherwise be implicit in naming conventions.
2570	
2571	[[axo_method]]
2572	{axo_method}::
2573	A
2574	{axl_motif}
2575	representing an executable operation affiliated with a specific
2576	{axo_entity}.
2577	Peer to
2578	{axo_procedure};
2579	both are kinds of operation, neither voices the other.
2580	The entity must appear as the second
2581	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2582	in the definition text.
2583	This is a grammar constraint: the voicing demands the relationship be stated.
2584	
2585	== Axial Hierarchy Operation Markers
2586	
2587	{axho*} markers appear at the
2588	{axl_detail_site}
2589	as standalone annotations, paralleling the
2590	{axhr*}
2591	regime hierarchy markers.
2592	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
2593	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2594	from the following text.
2595	
2596	=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2597	
2598	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2599	|===
2600	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2601	
2602	|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
2603	|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
2604	|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
2605	|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
2606	|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2607	|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
2608	|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2609	|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2610	|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2611	|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
2612	|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
2613	|===
2614	
2615	[[axhob_operation]]
2616	{axhob_operation}::
2617	Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
2618	{axl_detail_site}.
2619	Lookahead: 1
2620	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2621	— the operation this detail site describes.
2622	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2623	{axvo_procedure},
2624	{axvo_method},
2625	or
2626	{axvo_tabtarget}
2627	at the
2628	{axl_definition_site}.
2629	No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2630	{axl_definition_site}
2631	only.
2632	
2633	The surface syntax:
2634	
2635	[source,asciidoc]
2636	----
2637	//axhob_operation
2638	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
2639	
2640	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2641	{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
2642	----
2643	
2644	[[axhoc_completion]]
2645	{axhoc_completion}::
2646	Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
2647	{axl_detail_site}.
2648	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
2649	Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
2650	Distinct from
2651	{axhog_guarantee}
2652	which describes state promises, and
2653	{axhoot_typed_output}
2654	/{axhoom_motif_output}
2655	which describe produced data.
2656	One per operation.
2657	
2658	[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
2659	{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
2660	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2661	{axl_detail_site}
2662	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2663	No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
2664	Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
2665	Lookahead: 1
2666	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2667	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
2668	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2669	
2670	[[axhopa_arg_parameter]]
2671	{axhopa_arg_parameter}::
2672	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2673	{axl_detail_site}
2674	whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
2675	The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
2676	Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
2677	Lookahead: 1
2678	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2679	— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
2680	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2681	
2682	[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]
2683	{axhopm_motif_parameter}::
2684	Marks an input parameter in an operation
2685	{axl_detail_site}
2686	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2687	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2688	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2689	
2690	[[axhoot_typed_output]]
2691	{axhoot_typed_output}::
2692	Marks a named output in an operation
2693	{axl_detail_site}
2694	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2695	Parallel to the
2696	{axhopt_typed_parameter}
2697	pattern: no new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
2698	Used when the output's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., an output that is simply a list of names or a CIDR block).
2699	Lookahead: 1
2700	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2701	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
2702	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2703	
2704	[[axhoom_motif_output]]
2705	{axhoom_motif_output}::
2706	Marks an output in an operation
2707	{axl_detail_site}
2708	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2709	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2710	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2711	+
2712	May carry
2713	{axd_fact}
2714	to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
2715	{axd_tally}
2716	to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.
2717	
2718	[[axhoq_precondition]]
2719	{axhoq_precondition}::
2720	Marks a required precondition in an operation
2721	{axl_detail_site}.
2722	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2723	Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.
2724	
2725	[[axhog_guarantee]]
2726	{axhog_guarantee}::
2727	Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
2728	{axl_detail_site}.
2729	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2730	Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.
2731	
2732	[[axhos_step]]
2733	{axhos_step}::
2734	Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
2735	{axl_detail_site}.
2736	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2737	Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
2738	Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
2739	{axhos_waymark}
2740	anchor.
2741	
2742	[[axhos_waymark]]
2743	{axhos_waymark}::
2744	Variant of
2745	{axhos_step}
2746	for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
2747	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2748	Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
2749	Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
2750	The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.
2751	
2752	=== Nesting Rules
2753	
2754	Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2755	
2756	* {axhob_operation}
2757	opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
2758	{axhob_operation}
2759	* {axhoc_completion},
2760	{axhopt_typed_parameter},
2761	{axhopa_arg_parameter},
2762	{axhopm_motif_parameter},
2763	{axhoot_typed_output},
2764	{axhoom_motif_output},
2765	{axhoq_precondition},
2766	{axhog_guarantee},
2767	and
2768	{axhos_step}
2769	must appear within
2770	{axhob_operation}
2771	scope
2772	* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
2773	* At most one
2774	{axhoc_completion}
2775	per operation
2776	
2777	=== Cross-Document Validation
2778	
2779	* {axhob_operation}
2780	attribute reference must match an operation defined with
2781	{axvo_procedure},
2782	{axvo_method},
2783	or
2784	{axvo_tabtarget}
2785	at the
2786	{axl_definition_site}
2787	* {axhob_operation}
2788	carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2789	{axl_definition_site}
2790	only
2791	* When a
2792	{axvo_method}
2793	uses
2794	{axd_grouped},
2795	the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity
2796	
2797	== Axial Hierarchy Protocol Markers
2798	
2799	{axhp*} markers appear at the
2800	{axl_detail_site}
2801	of an
2802	{axi_protocol}
2803	as standalone annotations, paralleling the
2804	{axho*}
2805	operation hierarchy markers.
2806	A protocol's structural detail — the roster of transactions, and within each transaction the call envelope and return envelope — is expressed through a four-level nesting of these markers.
2807	
2808	=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2809	
2810	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2811	|===
2812	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2813	
2814	|{axhpb_protocol}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The protocol this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
2815	|{axhpt_transaction}|0                            |A named call/return pairing within the protocol; bare marker, identity carried through heading and prose
2816	|{axhpc_call}       |0                            |The caller-side envelope of an enclosing transaction; bare marker, members listed in following prose
2817	|{axhpr_return}     |0                            |The callee-side envelope of an enclosing transaction; bare marker, members listed in following prose
2818	|===
2819	
2820	[[axhpb_protocol]]
2821	{axhpb_protocol}::
2822	Opens the protocol hierarchy block at the
2823	{axl_detail_site}.
2824	Lookahead: 1
2825	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2826	— the protocol this detail site describes.
2827	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2828	{axi_protocol}
2829	at the
2830	{axl_definition_site}.
2831	No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions (transport, lifecycle, concurrency) are authoritative at the
2832	{axl_definition_site}
2833	only.
2834	
2835	The surface syntax:
2836	
2837	[source,asciidoc]
2838	----
2839	//axhpb_protocol
2840	{apcswp_wire}
2841	
2842	//axhpt_transaction
2843	discerner — main detection transaction.
2844	
2845	//axhpc_call
2846	caller payload members:
2847	----
2848	
2849	[[axhpt_transaction]]
2850	{axhpt_transaction}::
2851	Marks a named call/return pairing within an
2852	{axi_protocol}
2853	detail site.
2854	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2855	A transaction groups exactly one
2856	{axhpc_call}
2857	and one
2858	{axhpr_return}
2859	that together constitute a single caller-initiated exchange.
2860	Transaction identity is carried through section heading and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
2861	Multiple
2862	{axhpt_transaction}
2863	blocks within one protocol describe the full set of exchanges the protocol supports.
2864	
2865	[[axhpc_call]]
2866	{axhpc_call}::
2867	Marks the caller-side envelope of an enclosing
2868	{axhpt_transaction}.
2869	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2870	The prose under this marker enumerates the envelope's members, each referencing a type term defined at the
2871	{axl_definition_site}
2872	(in the parent spec or a shared vocabulary).
2873	Exactly one
2874	{axhpc_call}
2875	per
2876	{axhpt_transaction}.
2877	
2878	[[axhpr_return]]
2879	{axhpr_return}::
2880	Marks the callee-side envelope of an enclosing
2881	{axhpt_transaction}.
2882	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2883	The prose under this marker enumerates the envelope's members using the same type-reference pattern as
2884	{axhpc_call}.
2885	Exactly one
2886	{axhpr_return}
2887	per
2888	{axhpt_transaction}.
2889	A transaction where the caller expects no data back still declares
2890	{axhpr_return}
2891	(with prose noting "empty envelope" or equivalent) — the return presence is the signal, and uniform structure keeps the hierarchy complete.
2892	
2893	=== Nesting Rules
2894	
2895	Protocol hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2896	
2897	* {axhpb_protocol}
2898	opens the protocol block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
2899	{axhpb_protocol}
2900	* {axhpt_transaction}
2901	must appear within
2902	{axhpb_protocol}
2903	scope
2904	* {axhpc_call}
2905	and
2906	{axhpr_return}
2907	must appear within
2908	{axhpt_transaction}
2909	scope, each exactly once
2910	* Transactions appear in document order; no required ordering is imposed, but convention places readiness or probe transactions first and main-workload transactions second
2911	
2912	=== Cross-Document Validation
2913	
2914	* {axhpb_protocol}
2915	attribute reference must match a protocol defined with
2916	{axi_protocol}
2917	at the
2918	{axl_definition_site}
2919	* {axhpb_protocol}
2920	carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2921	{axl_definition_site}
2922	only
2923	* Types referenced in
2924	{axhpc_call}
2925	and
2926	{axhpr_return}
2927	member prose must resolve to
2928	{axt_type}
2929	anchors in the parent spec or a shared vocabulary
2930	
2931	== Axial Voicing Entity Terms
2932	
2933	Three annotation families enable entity specification across
2934	{axl_definition_site_s}
2935	and
2936	{axl_detail_site_s},
2937	paralleling the regime and operation annotation patterns.
2938	
2939	Entity specifications describe objects with both structure (fields) and behavior (methods).
2940	Methods within an entity may be *scoped* or *elevated*:
2941	
2942	* A *scoped* method is defined entirely within the entity subdocument.
2943	It has no parent-document linked term — its identity exists only within the entity's contract, expressed through position and prose.
2944	
2945	* An *elevated* method possesses a linked term at the
2946	{axl_definition_site}
2947	in the parent document, voiced with
2948	{axvo_method}.
2949	Elevated methods earn independent addressability when other spec layers (procedures, dispatch protocols) need to reference them.
2950	
2951	A well-constrained entity is its own documentation.
2952	Scoped methods are the default; elevation is the exception for methods that must be independently addressable.
2953	
2954	=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axve_*)
2955	
2956	{axve_*} annotations appear at the
2957	{axl_definition_site}
2958	between anchor and definition, declaring that the term defines an entity.
2959	
2960	[[axve_entity]]
2961	{axve_entity}::
2962	Voices an
2963	{axo_entity}
2964	definition.
2965	The annotated term defines an object with both structure and behavior whose contract is elaborated in a subdocument via
2966	{axhe*}
2967	hierarchy markers.
2968	The first
2969	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2970	is the entity being defined.
2971	+
2972	{axd_dimension_s}:
2973	optional
2974	{axd_internal}.
2975	
2976	=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhe*_)
2977	
2978	Hierarchy markers appear in entity subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
2979	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form.
2980	Markers with non-zero arity read ahead a specified number of
2981	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2982	from the following text.
2983	Zero-arity markers carry their type and dimension information entirely on the annotation line.
2984	
2985	The entity hierarchy uses a two-layer structure:
2986	
2987	* *Layer 1*: Entity scope opened by
2988	{axheb_entity},
2989	containing field markers
2990	({axheft_typed_field},
2991	{axhefm_motif_field})
2992	and method markers
2993	({axhems_scoped_method},
2994	{axheme_elevated_method}).
2995	
2996	* *Layer 2*: Method scope opened by a method marker, containing parameter markers
2997	({axhempt_typed_parameter},
2998	{axhempa_arg_parameter},
2999	{axhempm_motif_parameter})
3000	and output markers
3001	({axhemot_typed_output},
3002	{axhemom_motif_output}).
3003	
3004	Variant suffixes identify the source of type identity:
3005	
3006	* `_typed_*` — 1-arity; lookahead reads a project-specific type term
3007	* `_arg_*` — 1-arity; lookahead reads a shared argument term
3008	* `_motif_*` — 0-arity; type expressed through axial motif terms on the annotation line
3009	
3010	==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
3011	
3012	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
3013	|===
3014	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
3015	
3016	|{axheb_entity}             |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The entity this subdoc describes
3017	|{axheft_typed_field}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A field's type (domain type term)
3018	|{axhefm_motif_field}       |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
3019	|{axhems_scoped_method}     |1 {mcm_inlay}                |Implementation method name; opens Layer 2
3020	|{axheme_elevated_method}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The parent-document method term
3021	|{axhempt_typed_parameter}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A parameter's type (domain type term)
3022	|{axhempa_arg_parameter}    |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A parameter's identity (shared argument term)
3023	|{axhempm_motif_parameter}  |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
3024	|{axhemot_typed_output}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An output's type (domain type term)
3025	|{axhemom_motif_output}     |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms)
3026	|===
3027	
3028	[[axheb_entity]]
3029	{axheb_entity}::
3030	Opens the entity hierarchy block at the
3031	{axl_detail_site}.
3032	Lookahead: 1
3033	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3034	— the entity this detail site describes.
3035	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
3036	{axve_entity}
3037	at the
3038	{axl_definition_site}.
3039	
3040	[[axheft_typed_field]]
3041	{axheft_typed_field}::
3042	Marks a data member of the entity whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
3043	Lookahead: 1
3044	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3045	— must resolve to an existing type anchor.
3046	{axd_dimension_s}
3047	on the annotation line qualify the field:
3048	{axd_required}
3049	or
3050	{axd_optional},
3051	{axd_repeated},
3052	etc.
3053	
3054	[[axhefm_motif_field]]
3055	{axhefm_motif_field}::
3056	Marks a data member of the entity whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
3057	0-arity — no lookahead.
3058	{axd_dimension_s}
3059	on the annotation line include a type motif
3060	({axt_string},
3061	{axt_boolean},
3062	{axt_array},
3063	etc.) and structural qualifiers
3064	({axd_required},
3065	{axd_repeated},
3066	etc.).
3067	
3068	[[axhems_scoped_method]]
3069	{axhems_scoped_method}::
3070	Opens a scoped method scope within the entity.
3071	The method has no parent-document
3072	{mcm_quoin}
3073	— its identity is an
3074	{mcm_inlay}
3075	that exists only within the entity subdocument.
3076	Lookahead: 1
3077	{mcm_inlay}
3078	— the implementation method name, recognized by its prefix pattern.
3079	Opens Layer 2 scope for parameter and output markers.
3080	
3081	[[axheme_elevated_method]]
3082	{axheme_elevated_method}::
3083	References an elevated method within the entity — a method that has a linked term at the
3084	{axl_definition_site}
3085	in the parent document, annotated with
3086	{axvo_method}.
3087	Lookahead: 1
3088	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3089	— the parent-document method term.
3090	Opens Layer 2 scope for parameter and output markers.
3091	
3092	[[axhempt_typed_parameter]]
3093	{axhempt_typed_parameter}::
3094	Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
3095	Lookahead: 1
3096	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3097	— must resolve to an existing type anchor.
3098	{axd_dimension_s}
3099	on the annotation line qualify the parameter:
3100	{axd_required}
3101	or
3102	{axd_optional},
3103	etc.
3104	
3105	[[axhempa_arg_parameter]]
3106	{axhempa_arg_parameter}::
3107	Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose identity reuses a shared argument term.
3108	Lookahead: 1
3109	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3110	— must resolve to an existing shared argument anchor.
3111	
3112	[[axhempm_motif_parameter]]
3113	{axhempm_motif_parameter}::
3114	Marks a parameter within the current method scope whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
3115	0-arity — no lookahead.
3116	{axd_dimension_s}
3117	on the annotation line include a type motif and structural qualifiers.
3118	
3119	[[axhemot_typed_output]]
3120	{axhemot_typed_output}::
3121	Marks an output of the current method scope whose type identity reuses an existing domain type term.
3122	Lookahead: 1
3123	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3124	— must resolve to an existing type anchor.
3125	
3126	[[axhemom_motif_output]]
3127	{axhemom_motif_output}::
3128	Marks an output of the current method scope whose type is expressed entirely through axial motif terms on the annotation line.
3129	0-arity — no lookahead.
3130	{axd_dimension_s}
3131	on the annotation line include a type motif and structural qualifiers.
3132	
3133	==== Nesting Rules
3134	
3135	Entity hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
3136	
3137	* {axheb_entity}
3138	opens the entity block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end
3139	* {axheft_typed_field}
3140	and
3141	{axhefm_motif_field}
3142	must appear within
3143	{axheb_entity}
3144	scope but outside any method scope (Layer 1)
3145	* {axhems_scoped_method}
3146	and
3147	{axheme_elevated_method}
3148	must appear within
3149	{axheb_entity}
3150	scope (Layer 1); each opens a new method scope (Layer 2)
3151	* A method marker implicitly closes any prior method scope
3152	* {axhempt_typed_parameter},
3153	{axhempa_arg_parameter},
3154	{axhempm_motif_parameter},
3155	{axhemot_typed_output},
3156	and
3157	{axhemom_motif_output}
3158	must appear within a method scope (Layer 2)
3159	* Field markers should precede method markers within the entity (fields then methods)
3160	
3161	==== Cross-Document Validation
3162	
3163	* {axheb_entity}
3164	attribute reference must match an entity defined with
3165	{axve_entity}
3166	at the
3167	{axl_definition_site}
3168	* {axheme_elevated_method}
3169	attribute reference must match a method defined with
3170	{axvo_method}
3171	at the
3172	{axl_definition_site},
3173	and that method's affiliated entity must match the entity opened by
3174	{axheb_entity}
3175	
3176	== Axial Type Universal Terms
3177	
3178	{axtu_*} types represent generic infrastructure patterns that are not specific to any cloud provider or platform.
3179	These types capture critical gestalts that appear across many systems.
3180	Platform-specific nuances (e.g., path separators on Windows vs Unix) are handled through
3181	{axl_voicing_s}.

3184	{axtu_string}::
3185	A
3186	{axl_motif}

3243	== Axial Type Google Terms
3244	
3245	{axtg_*} types represent Google Cloud Platform resource identifiers and patterns.

3259	{axl_motif}
