== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_handler} functions within {jjdx_vvx}.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: Rust {jjdx_handler} functions replace fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

Git has the full history of every mutation.
Plan evolution is visible through commit diffs without additional in-JSON bookkeeping.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Every operation follows the same invariant regardless of transport:
lock → load → transform → save → unlock.
+
The {jjdx_handler} acquires a git-ref lock (`git update-ref`),
loads the {jjdgr_gallops} JSON,
performs the transformation,
writes atomically (temp file then rename),
and releases the lock.
If the {jjdx_handler} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
+
No in-memory {jjdgr_gallops} state persists between operations.
Each operation reads from disk and writes back.
This is critical for the {jjdx_mcp}, which is a long-lived process:
caching state between calls would produce stale reads when multiple sessions write concurrently.

=== MCP Transport

[[jjdx_mcp]]
//axvo_procedure axd_longrunning
{jjdx_mcp}::
The `vvx mcp` subcommand starts a long-lived stdio server implementing the Model Context Protocol (MCP) specification version 2025-11-25.
The server communicates via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
Implemented using the `rmcp` crate (official Rust MCP SDK), version pinned exactly.
+
The {jjdx_mcp} process is stateless infrastructure — it does not manage {jjdxo_officium} lifecycle.
{jjdxo_officium_s} are created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}), not by server process startup.
See <<jjdxo_officium>> for lifecycle details.

[[jjdx_tool]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{jjdx_tool}::
A JSON-RPC tool call within an {jjdx_mcp} session.
Each operation is registered as an {jjdx_tool} with typed parameter schemas and descriptions.
{jjdx_tool_s} are the sole invocation path for Claude Code.
The {jjdx_cli} CLI subcommand path is removed;
{jjdx_tool_s} are NOT also available as CLI subcommands.
+
Infrastructure commands ({jjdx_vvx} emplace, unlock) remain CLI-only.

[[jjdx_handler]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{jjdx_handler}::
The transport-agnostic Rust function that implements an operation.
Both {jjdx_tool_s} and (historical) CLI subcommands invoke the same {jjdx_handler}.
{jjdx_handler} functions accept typed parameters and return structured results;
the transport layer (MCP or CLI) handles serialization.

Each {jjdx_tool} call is stateless: the {jjdx_handler} performs the full crash-safe invariant (lock → load → transform → save → unlock) per invocation.
The {jjdx_mcp} holds no {jjdgr_gallops} state between calls.
+
Operations that previously read text from stdin (
{jjdo_enroll},
{jjsoprd_revise_docket},
{jjdo_transfer},
{jjdo_close},
{jjdo_landing})
accept structured string parameters instead.
The {jjdx_mcp} resolves the {jjdgr_gallops} path internally;
{jjda_file} is not exposed as a tool parameter.

=== Officium Lifecycle

[[jjdxo_officium]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_officium}::
A bounded agent session with a unique identity and a dedicated filesystem directory.
Each chat session has exactly one {jjdxo_officium}, created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}).
+
The {jjdxo_officium} is decoupled from {jjdx_mcp} process lifetime.
The {jjdx_mcp} is stateless disposable infrastructure — it may restart at any time without affecting the {jjdxo_officium}.
The agent (chat session) is the stable identity anchor; the {jjdxo_officium} ID is a routing parameter passed on every {jjdx_tool} call.
+
The term derives from the Latin _Officium Divinum_ (Divine Office) — a structured, bounded period of intentional work.
+
Multiple {jjdxo_officium_s} may coexist concurrently (multiple Claude Code windows).
Per {jjdk_sole_operator}, all belong to a single operator.
The commit lock serializes their mutations; the {jjdxo_exchange} isolates their gazette file I/O.

==== Identity

Each {jjdxo_officium} is assigned a unique identifier by {jjdo_open}.
Format: `YYMMDD-NNNN` where `YYMMDD` is the local datestamp and `NNNN` is an autonumber (enumerate existing directories for the date, pick next unused starting at 1000).
No persistent counter file — the directory listing is the source of truth.
+
Unicode verification prefix: ☉ (U+2609 SUN, evoking the Divine Office's daily cycle).
The ☉ appears in parameters and display; it is stripped for the directory name.
This parallels the ₣/₢ convention for {jjdt_firemark_s} and {jjdt_coronet_s}.
+
The identifier appears in the {jjdxo_exchange} path and in the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit.

==== Directory Layout

Each {jjdxo_officium} creates a directory via {jjdo_open}:
+
----
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_in.md   (ephemeral: agent → server)
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_out.md  (ephemeral: server → agent)
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/heartbeat
----
+
The `officia/` directory is the parent for all active {jjdxo_officium_s}.
Gazette files (`gazette_in.md`, `gazette_out.md`) have single-MCP-call lifetime — they are parameters and return values, not persistent state.
The {jjdxo_heartbeat} file provides liveness detection.
+
The entire `.claude/jjm/officia/` tree is gitignored — it is ephemeral exchange infrastructure, not tracked state.
This eliminates git noise from officium operations and makes incorrect {jjdxo_exsanguination} benign (no tracked files lost).
+
Directories that survive session termination are stale and are reaped by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.

[[jjdxo_exchange]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_exchange}::
The per-{jjdxo_officium} directory (`.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/`) used for {jjsz_gazette} file exchange between {jjdx_tool_s} and Claude.
+
Only one {jjsz_gazette} file exists at a time: {jjdxo_gazette}.
Operations that produce gazette output write to this file; Claude reads it.
Claude writes gazette input to this file; operations that accept gazette input read it.
+
The sequential nature of tool calls (one at a time per session) means the single file never has concurrent readers and writers within one {jjdxo_officium}.

[[jjdxo_gazette]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_gazette}::
Two directional gazette files within an {jjdxo_exchange}: `gazette_in.md` (agent to server) and `gazette_out.md` (server to agent).
Contain {jjsz_gazette} wire-format markdown.
Both have single-MCP-call lifetime — every {jjdx_tool_s} call deletes both files on entry before dispatch.
+
`gazette_in.md` is written by Claude before setter operations ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjsoprd_revise_docket}, {jjdo_paddock} setter).
`gazette_out.md` is written by the server after getter operations ({jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_show}, {jjdo_paddock} getter).

[[jjdxo_invitatory]]
//axvo_procedure axd_internal
{jjdxo_invitatory}::
The officium creation procedure.
Triggered by the agent calling {jjdo_open} — not by {jjdx_mcp} process startup.
The agent calls {jjdo_open} once per chat session.
+
{jjds_behavior}
+
. Run {jjdxo_exsanguination} (reap stale officia before creating new one)
. Generate {jjdxo_officium} identity (datestamp + autonumber)
. Create {jjdxo_exchange} directory with {jjdxo_gazette} and {jjdxo_heartbeat} files (both empty)
. Resolve current Claude Code session UUID by listing `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl` and selecting newest mtime (encoding rule: `/` and `_` both map to `-` in the absolute cwd path); fail loud on missing dir, zero `.jsonl` files, or mtime tie
. Collect model inventory via `vvcp_probe()` if daily probe has not yet run (check `.claude/jjm/officia/.probe_date` datestamp file)
. Create invitatory commit (action code `i`, `jjb`-branded) with `session:` + `cwd:` lines always present in body, optionally followed by model inventory when the daily probe ran
. Return {jjdxo_officium} identity string (e.g. `☉260327-1000`)
+
The agent receives the identity and passes it as a parameter on all subsequent {jjdx_tool} calls.

[[jjdxo_compline]]
//axve_entity axd_retired
{jjdxo_compline}::
Retired.
The original design called for a clean shutdown procedure at {jjdx_mcp} process termination.
This proved unworkable: {jjdx_mcp} processes restart at unpredictable frequency, decoupled from chat session boundaries.
+
Staleness is now detected by {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime absence.
Cleanup is performed by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.
Sessions simply stop — no compline runs.
+
The action code `o` is reserved-unused.

[[jjdxo_heartbeat]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_heartbeat}::
A sentinel file within the {jjdxo_exchange} directory: `heartbeat`.
Every {jjdx_tool} dispatch touches this file (updates mtime).
+
The {jjdxo_heartbeat} provides liveness detection without coupling to process trees or Claude Code internals.
A stale {jjdxo_heartbeat} (mtime exceeding the staleness threshold) indicates the owning session has ended.
{jjdxo_exsanguination} uses {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime to identify directories safe to reap.

[[jjdxo_exsanguination]]
//axvo_procedure axd_internal
{jjdxo_exsanguination}::
The stale officium reaping procedure.
Runs at the beginning of each {jjdxo_invitatory}, before creating the new {jjdxo_officium} directory.
Returns counts of active and reaped officia for {jjdo_open} summary output.
+
{jjds_behavior}
+
. Scan `.claude/jjm/officia/` for directories (skip hidden entries)
. For each directory, check {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime
. Remove directories whose {jjdxo_heartbeat} exceeds the staleness threshold (7 days); count as reaped
. Count directories within threshold as active
. Skip directories with no {jjdxo_heartbeat} file (legacy or corrupt — log warning, leave for manual cleanup)
. Return (active count, reaped count)
+
The week-long staleness threshold ensures all same-day directories survive long enough for the autonumber scan to see them, preventing {jjdxo_officium} identity collisions within a day.
Because the `officia/` tree is gitignored, incorrect reaping is benign — no tracked files are lost.

==== Steeplechase Commit Patterns

{jjdxo_invitatory} commit (action code `i`):
+
----
jjb:BRAND::i: OFFICIUM <id>

session: <uuid>
cwd: <absolute-path>
haiku: <model-id>
sonnet: <model-id>
opus: <model-id>
host: <hostname>
platform: <platform>
----
+
The `session:` and `cwd:` lines appear in *every* invitatory body — each chat session has its own UUID and may run from a different cwd, so the recall anchor must be present on every {jjdo_open}.
The model/host/platform inventory below them appears only on the first {jjdxo_invitatory} of the day (gated by `.probe_date`); subsequent same-day invitatories carry only `session:` + `cwd:`.
+
*Recall workflow.*
The `session:` and `cwd:` lines complete the chat-level recall loop:
+
* `git log --all --grep='OFFICIUM <id>'` — locate every commit from a given {jjdxo_officium} (subject anchor)
* `git log --all --grep='session: <uuid>'` — locate every commit from a given chat session (transcript anchor)
* To resume a chat: extract `session:` and `cwd:` from the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit body, change to that working directory, run `claude --resume <session-uuid>`
+
The `cwd:` line is recorded verbatim from the MCP server's process working directory at {jjdo_open} time — no symlink resolution, no case normalization. Recall is therefore machine-local; the transcript files under `~/.claude/projects/` are local anyway.
+
The invitatory commit uses empty identity (no firemark or coronet) — the officium is infrastructure, not affiliated with a heat.
+
The {jjdxo_compline} action code `o` is reserved-unused.
No compline commits are created — sessions end without ceremony.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_reorder}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
+
The `heats` object uses BTreeMap (alphabetical key order).
{jjdgm_order}
is the authoritative source for heat display and selection order.
Operations that need heat order (
{jjdo_list},
default heat selection in
{jjdo_orient})
iterate
{jjdgm_order},
not the `heats` object directly.

=== Identity Encoding

Job Jockey uses the
{jjdt_insignia}
type system for all entity identities.
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia_s}
({jjdt_firemark},
{jjdt_coronet},
{jjdt_pensum})
use the {jjdz_charset} providing compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Digit count disambiguates type, with one exception:
{jjdt_pensum}
shares digit count 5 with
{jjdt_coronet}
and uses a `%` sentinel at position 3 for discrimination.
+
{jjdxo_officium}
uses
{axd_temporal}
encoding (YYMMDD-NNNN).
+
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
See
{jjdt_insignia}
in the Types section for the complete identity table.

=== Remote Dispatch

Remote dispatch enables a
{jjdxr_curia}
(the invoking side — where the
{jjdxo_officium}
lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works)
to execute operations on a
{jjdxr_fundus}
(the executing side — where BUD runs, where BURX is written, where tabtargets physically execute).

[[jjdxr_curia]]
//axl_voices axo_role
{jjdxr_curia}::
The invoking side of a remote operation.
Where the
{jjdxo_officium}
lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works.
The administrative court from which
{jjdt_legatio_s}
are sent.
+
{jjdxr_curia}-side
state: the
{jjdxo_officium}
directory holds active
{jjdt_legatio}
handles and
{jjdt_pensum}
seeds (per
{jjdhr_heat},
in `pensum_seeds.json`);
pulled-back logs are archived by
{jjdt_pensum}
token.

[[jjdxr_fundus]]
//axl_voices axo_role
{jjdxr_fundus}::
The executing side of a remote operation.
Where BUD runs, where `burx.env` is written (per {xref_BUS0}), where the tabtarget physically executes.
Latin: _fundus_ — estate, landed property; the ground where labor happens.
+
{jjdxr_fundus}
preconditions: user account with SSH key access from
{jjdxr_curia},
project directory at `~user/RELDIR` with BUK installed,
functional BUD dispatch chain, git repo with origin configured.
+
{jjdxr_curia}-side
precondition: a {xref_BURN} for the target connection,
providing host, user, SSH alias, and public key material.
The alias is the addressing handle — all remote dispatch operations resolve
{jjdxr_fundus}
coordinates through the BURN profile.

==== Legatio and Pensum

Remote dispatch uses a two-tier identity model:

{jjdt_legatio}::
Session handle (connection descriptor).
The meaningful identity is the target pair (alias, reldir); the alias resolves host and user from a {xref_BURN}.
The token is
{jjdxo_officium}-scoped —
a convenience handle, not a persistent record.
See
{jjdt_legatio}
in the Types section.

{jjdxp_pensum}::
Job identity (dispatch record).
Globally unique
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia}
that correlates
{jjdxr_curia}-side
dispatch records with
{jjdxr_fundus}-side
logs via `BURX_LABEL`.
See
{jjdt_pensum}
in the Types section.

[[jjdxp_pensum]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxp_pensum}::
A single dispatched asynchronous operation on a
{jjdxr_fundus}.
Created by
{jjdo_relay},
probed by
{jjdo_check}.
Identified by a
{jjdt_pensum}
token that round-trips through `BURE_LABEL` on the
{jjdxr_fundus}
into `BURX_LABEL` in the
{xref_BUS0}
`burx.env` fact-file.
+
{jjdxp_pensum}
state on the
{jjdxr_curia}:
{jjdt_legatio}
reference and remote temp dir path (captured at relay time).
Multiple
{jjdxp_pensum_s}
may coexist within an
{jjdxo_officium}.

==== Pensum Report States

{jjdo_check}
returns a liveness report for each
{jjdxp_pensum}:

[[jjdxpe_running]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_running}::
`burx.env` present, PID alive, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
The dispatch is still executing.
Serialized as `running` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_orphaned]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_orphaned}::
`burx.env` present, PID dead, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
The dispatch crashed without writing terminal status.
Serialized as `orphaned` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_stopped]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_stopped}::
`BURX_EXIT_STATUS` present.
The dispatch completed; exit code available.
On terminal status,
{jjdo_check}
additionally returns the temp dir file list.
Serialized as `stopped` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_lost]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_lost}::
`burx.env` file missing.
The dispatch's temp dir was not found at the stored path.
Serialized as `lost` in pensum liveness report.

== Premises

[[jjdk_sole_operator]]
//axk_premise
{jjdk_sole_operator}::
All concurrent {jjdxo_officium_s} belong to a single operator.
The commit lock serializes operations across parallel {jjdxo_officium_s};
cross-user concurrency is out of scope.
+
Consequently, every operation acquires the lock unconditionally — there is no
read-only fast path, and the {jjsodp_command_lifecycle} dispatcher need not
distinguish mutating from non-mutating operations.
+
{jjdxo_officium_s} need only identification (which session), not authentication (who).

[[jjdk_reldir_safety]]
//axk_premise
{jjdk_reldir_safety}::
Remote dispatch involves destructive operations on the
{jjdxr_fundus}
(workspace cleanup via
{jjdo_plant}).
A malformed
{jjdt_reldir}
could resolve to `/` or `~`, destroying the filesystem or the user's home.
Three layers defend against this; no single layer is sufficient alone.
+
*Layer 1 — Rust constant validation (compile-time obvious):*
Must not be empty, must not start with `/` (absolute), must not start with `.` (traversal), must contain at least one `/` (forces subdirectory depth).
+
*Layer 2 — Fundus-side resolved-path check:*
The remote command validates the resolved path before any destructive operation: must be strictly under `$HOME`, must have minimum depth (at least 2 components under `$HOME`).
+
*Layer 3 — Bind-time probe:*
{jjdo_bind}
performs the same resolved-path check at session creation.
Fail fast before any
{jjdo_relay}
is ever attempted.

== Equestrian Vocabulary

=== Vocabulary Isolation

Job Jockey's terminology is drawn entirely from equestrianism.
This is not decoration — it is a namespace discipline applied to human cognition and language model disambiguation.

Planning systems face a persistent hazard: when the vocabulary used to _organize_ work overlaps with the vocabulary used to _do_ work, the boundary between plan and product dissolves.
"Task" could be a planning unit or a runtime construct.
"Phase" could be a workflow stage or a build step.
"Diff" could be a review artifact or a git object.
Every ambiguous term forces a context switch — the human pauses to resolve which layer a word belongs to, and the language model may silently resolve it wrong.

In early incarnations, Job Jockey used generic planning terms that bled directly into source code.
Planning concepts became load-bearing in two systems simultaneously: changing one risked breaking the other.
The fix was not better naming within the same domain — it was choosing a source domain so alien to software engineering that collision becomes structurally impossible.

Equestrianism shares zero terminology with software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, or corporate project management.
No codebase has a `heat` object.
No ticketing system uses `firemark`.
No CI step is called `corral`.
When a human says "mount," there is no ambiguity about which system they are addressing.
When a language model encounters "school," it does not reach for a compiler.

The vocabulary also exploits a property of human memory: concrete, physical, sensory metaphors lodge more durably than abstract labels.
A jockey wearing silks, a horse being schooled in a dressage ring, a volte circling back to the same point with refined intent — these images persist across sessions in ways that "human-claimed-pending-interactive-work" cannot.
The cognitive cost of re-establishing context drops when the terms are vivid and the metaphor is coherent.

=== Racing Vocabulary

The original layer, established in V3.
Terms drawn from horse racing — the competitive, forward-driving aspect of initiative management.

* {jjdhr_heat} — a bounded initiative. A race: defined start, defined finish, sustained effort.
* {jjdpr_pace} — a discrete action within a heat. The rhythm of hooves — each stride advances the race.
* {jjdt_firemark} — heat identity, `₣`-prefixed. The hot-iron brand that marks ownership on a living animal — permanent, unmistakable.
* {jjdt_coronet} — pace identity, `₢`-prefixed. The marking at the hoof line — fine-grained, below the firemark, identifying the individual step.
* {jjdt_silks} — display name. The jockey's colors — vivid, human-recognizable, worn for the crowd, not the horse.
* {jjdgr_gallops} — the persistent data store. Training grounds where real work happens before race day — not the race itself, but where readiness is built.
* *paddock* — heat-wide context. The gathering area where horses are assessed before entering the course.
* *muster* — list heats. Assembling the field before the race.
* *parade* — show heat or pace detail. The formal presentation of horses before the crowd.
* *furlough* — alter heat status. A horse temporarily withdrawn from racing — not retired, not scratched, resting.
* *retire* — archive a completed heat. The horse leaves the racing life — honored, not discarded.
* *rail* — reorder paces. The rail governs position on the track — who runs where.

=== Dressage Vocabulary

The V4 layer, introduced for the school-breeze-corral execution pipeline.
Terms drawn from dressage and classical horsemanship — the disciplined, precise, iterative aspect of training and evaluation.

* *school* — per-pace planning phase. Schooling a horse: patient, attentive work that develops capability before asking for performance. The rider (human) is fully present.
* *longe* — heat-level readiness assessment. Working a horse on a long line to assess soundness before mounting — observation without commitment.
* *breeze* — autonomous execution phase. A breeze work: the horse runs at controlled speed, demonstrating trained capability without the rider directing each stride.
* *corral* — review and integration phase. Gathering horses after work — assessing condition, accepting or turning back for more training.
* *volte* — an execution attempt covering one or more paces. A dressage figure: a precise, controlled circle back to the same point with refined intent. Each volte attempts the same work with accumulated understanding.
* *caracole* — volte identity, `₡`-prefixed. A cavalry half-turn — circling back for another pass at the same objective.
* *gait* — reusable recipe of beats. The trained movement patterns a horse carries — walk, trot, canter, each with distinct rhythm and purpose.
* *beat* — atomic execution unit. The indivisible footfall within a gait — one hoof striking ground.
* *quirt* — gait identity, `Ꝗ`-prefixed. A short riding whip — the thing that sets a gait in motion.
* *reined* — under human authority. The rider holds the reins; the pipeline does not touch this horse.

=== Presentation Aliases

The display and output layer uses distinct terms for certain schema fields, following the same vocabulary isolation principle:

* *docket* — the presentation term for the `text` field. What the {jjdpr_pace} should accomplish. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions.

These are display/CLI terms, not schema changes. Field renames are deferred to a future heat.

== Upper API

Job Jockey has two distinct API layers.
The *lower layer* is the {jjdx_tool} set: boring, heterogeneous verb names implemented in Rust ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_close}, etc.).
The *upper layer* is the equestrian vocabulary that humans use to direct a language model ({jjsuv_slate}, {jjsuv_mount}, {jjsuv_wrap}, etc.).

The separation is deliberate and follows the vocabulary isolation principle described in Equestrian Vocabulary.
Shared vocabulary between layers caused the LLM to conflate them — guessing tool parameters by analogy, reaching for raw tools instead of following workflows.
Boring lower-layer names resist pattern matching.
Vivid upper-layer names stay memorable for humans and unambiguous for language models.

The {jjsuc_insert} is the primary artifact that translates between layers — most verbs are delivered via the CLAUDE.md verb table.
V4 reserves slash commands for protocol-heavy orchestration (school, corral).
The human speaks equestrian vocabulary; the LLM invokes {jjdx_tool_s} via MCP.

Upper API verbs map equestrian vocabulary to {jjdx_tool_s}.
Some verbs map directly to a single {jjdx_tool}; others orchestrate multiple tools.
All verbs are delivered via the {jjsuc_insert} verb table.

=== Slash Commands

V4 reserves slash commands for protocol-heavy orchestration (school, corral).
These will be specified when implemented.
All other verbs are delivered via the {jjsuc_insert} verb table.

=== Context Artifact

[[jjsuc_insert]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_section
{jjsuc_insert}::
The `MANAGED:JJK` section of the project CLAUDE.md file.
Contains the quick verb mapping table, CLI command reference, and operational guidance that enables the LLM to translate upper API verbs into {jjdx_cli} invocations.
This section is the primary mechanism by which the upper and lower API layers are bridged at runtime.

== Types

=== Identity System

[[jjdt_insignia]]
//axl_voices axt_insignia
{jjdt_insignia}::
A glyph-prefixed typed identity string in Job Jockey's type system.
Every
{jjdt_insignia}
has four properties: a unicode verification glyph (decorative in input, mandatory in output), a fixed encoding format, a monotonic seed allocation mechanism, and optional hierarchical embedding (parent identity encoded as a prefix substring).
+
Two encoding natures, declared via dimension:
+
* {axd_algebraic} — {jjdz_charset} characters, fixed digit count, seed incremented per allocation ({jjdt_firemark}/{jjdt_coronet} under commit lock; {jjdt_pensum} in officium-local storage).
Digit count disambiguates type within the algebraic family, with one exception: {jjdt_pensum} shares digit count 5 with {jjdt_coronet} and uses a `%` sentinel at position 3 for disambiguation.
Case matters: the final character's case distinguishes identities.
+
* {axd_temporal} — datestamp + autonumber (YYMMDD-NNNN), allocated by filesystem enumeration.
No hierarchical embedding.
+
[cols="1,2,1,1,3,2"]
|===
| Symbol | Name | Unicode | Digits | Structure | Capacity

| ₣ | {jjdt_firemark}   | U+20A3 | 2 | 2 heat                               | 4,096 heats
| ₢ | {jjdt_coronet}    | U+20A2 | 5 | 2 heat + 3 index                     | ~262K per heat
| ₱ | {jjdt_pensum}     | U+20B1 | 5 | 2 heat + `%` sentinel + 2 index      | 4,096 per heat
| ☉ | {jjdxo_officium}  | U+2609 | — | YYMMDD-NNNN                          | temporal
|===

=== Algebraic {jjdt_insignia_s}

[[jjdt_firemark]]
//axl_voices axt_insignia axd_algebraic
{jjdt_firemark}::
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia}
for
{jjdhr_heat}
identity.
Format: `₣` + 2 {jjdz_charset} characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
Seed:
{jjdgm_seed}
on
{jjdgr_gallops},
incremented by
{jjdo_create}.
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
//axl_voices axt_insignia axd_algebraic
{jjdt_coronet}::
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia}
for
{jjdpr_pace}
identity.
Format: `₢` + 5 {jjdz_charset} characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
{jjdt_firemark};
the remaining 3 encode the pace index.
Seed:
{jjdhm_seed}
on
{jjdhr_heat},
incremented by
{jjdo_enroll}.
Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
+
*Reassignment:* When a
{jjdpr_pace}
is moved between
{jjdhr_heat_s}
via
{jjdo_relocate},
the old
{jjdt_coronet}
becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
{jjdhr_heat_p}
{jjdhm_seed}.
The new
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds the destination
{jjdt_firemark}.
Pace data is preserved, with a note recording the original
{jjdt_coronet}.

[[jjdt_pensum]]
//axl_voices axt_insignia axd_algebraic
{jjdt_pensum}::
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia}
for remote dispatch job identity.
Format: `₱` + 2 {jjdz_charset} (heat
{jjdt_firemark})
+ `%` sentinel + 2 {jjdz_charset} (index).
Example: `₱Ah%BE` belongs to
{jjdhr_heat}
`₣Ah`.
+
The `%` sentinel at position 3 disambiguates from
{jjdt_coronet},
which shares digit count 5.
`%` cannot appear in the {jjdz_charset}, so the parse is mechanical: 5-character identity with `%` at position 3 is
{jjdt_pensum},
without is
{jjdt_coronet}.
+
Seed:
{jjdhm_pensum_seed}
in the
{jjdxo_officium}
directory (keyed by
{jjdt_firemark}),
incremented by
{jjdo_relay}.
+
*Persistence:*
{jjdt_pensum}
is a globally unique correlation key.
It appears in `BURX_LABEL` on the
{jjdxr_fundus}
(via `BURE_LABEL` in the {xref_BUS0}) and indexes
{jjdxr_curia}-side
log archives.
Its primary purpose is fossil-record correlation — every remote dispatch is traceable months later by its
{jjdt_pensum}
token.
+
*Heat affiliation:* Every
{jjdt_pensum}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(characters 0-1).
This correlates remote dispatches to initiative work without requiring pace affiliation.
A
{jjdt_pensum}
dispatched during housekeeping with no active
{jjdpr_pace}
still gets a valid identity.

=== Structural Types

[[jjdt_legatio]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{jjdt_legatio}::
A session-local convenience handle for an SSH connection to a remote project.
The meaningful identity is the target pair: (alias, reldir); the alias resolves host and user from a {xref_BURN}.
The
{jjdt_legatio}
token is an
{jjdxo_officium}-scoped
handle (e.g., `L0`, `L1`) that avoids repeating the triple on every
{jjdo_send},
{jjdo_relay},
{jjdo_fetch},
or
{jjdo_plant}
call.
+
{jjdt_legatio}
is NOT a globally allocated
{jjdt_insignia} —
it has no unicode glyph, no persistent seed, and no fossil-record purpose.
Queries against dispatch history use the target pair, not the
{jjdt_legatio}
token.
Created by
{jjdo_bind};
destroyed by
{jjdxo_officium}
absolution.

[[jjdt_reldir]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{jjdt_reldir}::
A relative directory path from the
{jjdxr_fundus}
user's home directory to the project root.
SSH sessions land in `~` by default, so `cd $RELDIR && ...` works across platforms without the
{jjdxr_curia}
knowing the absolute home path.
+
Validation rules (enforced by
{jjdk_reldir_safety}):
must not be empty, must not start with `/`, must not start with `.`, must contain at least one `/`.
+
Example: `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.

=== Other Types

[[jjdt_silks]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{jjdt_silks}::
A case-insensitive kebab display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-zA-Z0-9]+(-[a-zA-Z0-9]+)*`

[[jjdt_slug]]
//axl_voices axt_enumeration
{jjdt_slug}::
The category identifier for
{jjsz_gazette}
notices.
Values are the
{jjezs_slate},
{jjezs_reslate},
{jjezs_paddock},
and
{jjezs_pace}
constants defined in the
{jjsz_gazette}
subdocument.

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
//axl_voices axr_record_json
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_create}.
Serialized as `jjgrn_next_heat_seed` in JSON.

* [[jjdgm_order]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated axd_property
{jjdgm_order}
is the ordered list of
{jjdt_firemark_s}
defining heat display and selection order.
Serialized as `jjgrn_heat_order` in JSON.
Populated from sorted
{jjdgm_heats}
keys on first load of old-format files (migration);
thereafter maintained explicitly.
Updated by:
{jjdo_create}
(append new firemark),
{jjdo_archive}
(remove firemark via retain),
{jjdo_continue}
(move new firemark to position 0).
{jjdgm_order}
is the authoritative source for display and selection order, not
{jjdgm_heats}
key order.

* [[jjdgm_version]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_optional axt_number axd_property
{jjdgm_version}
is the schema version number.
Absent in V3 files; present in V4+ as an unsigned integer.
Serialized as `jjgrn_schema_version` in JSON.
{jjdr_load}
detects migration mode when absent or when
{jjdgm_order}
is empty.

* [[jjdgm_heats]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_map axd_property
{jjdgm_heats}
{jjdt_firemark}
{jjdhr_heat}
is the dictionary of all
{jjdhr_heat_s}
keyed by
{jjdt_firemark}.
Serialized as `jjgrn_heats` in JSON.
Serialized with lexicographic key order for deterministic output.
Display and selection order governed by
{jjdgm_order},
not key order.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
//axl_voices axr_record_json
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_positional
{jjdhm_firemark}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
primary key.
Type:
{jjdt_firemark}.
Encoded as the JSON map key of this Heat's entry in
{jjdgm_heats};
no separate property name.

* [[jjdhm_silks]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
{jjdhm_silks}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.
Serialized as `jjghn_silks` in JSON.

* [[jjdhm_created]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
{jjdhm_created}
records when the
{jjdhr_heat}
was nominated.
Format: YYMMDD.
Serialized as `jjghn_creation_time` in JSON.

* [[jjdhm_status]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
{jjdhm_status}
indicates the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
lifecycle state.
Values:
{jjdhe_racing},
{jjdhe_stabled},
or
{jjdhe_retired}.
Serialized as `jjghn_status` in JSON.

* [[jjdhm_order]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated axd_property
{jjdhm_order}
lists
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in execution sequence.
May be reordered via
{jjdo_reorder}.
Serialized as `jjghn_order` in JSON.

* [[jjdhm_seed]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
{jjdhm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_coronet}
index to allocate, stored as 3 {jjdz_charset} characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_enroll}.
Serialized as `jjghn_next_pace_seed` in JSON.

* [[jjdhm_pensum_seed]]
{jjdhm_pensum_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_pensum}
index to allocate, stored as 2 {jjdz_charset} characters.
Lives in the
{jjdxo_officium}
directory as `pensum_seeds.json` (keyed by
{jjdt_firemark}).
Incremented by
{jjdo_relay}.
Initialized to `AA` on first relay for a given heat within an officium.
+
Formerly a
{jjdhr_heat}
member in
{jjdgr_gallops}.
Moved to officium-local storage because each mint committed to gallops, advancing HEAD past origin and triggering the curia readiness guard on subsequent relays.
Pensum tokens are session-scoped ephemeral correlation labels — they do not belong in permanent project history.
Cross-officium seed collision is acceptable.

* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
{jjdhm_paddock}
is the path to the paddock file containing
{jjdhr_heat}
context and background.
Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_{encoded}.md` where `{encoded}` is the
{jjdt_firemark}
(without `₣` prefix) with each character prefixed by `u` (uppercase) or `l`
(lowercase) to ensure uniqueness on case-insensitive filesystems (macOS
HFS+/APFS).
Examples: firemark `AG` → `jjp_uAuG.md`; firemark `Ag` → `jjp_uAlg.md`.
Computed on demand from the
{jjdhm_firemark}
via `jjri_paddock_path`; not persisted in
{jjdgr_gallops}.
The paddock file itself is created by
{jjdo_create}.
+
Formerly a
{jjdhr_heat}
member in
{jjdgr_gallops}.
Removed because the value is fully determined by the
{jjdhm_firemark}
— storing it in JSON was redundant noise that drifted from the encoding rule
during the case-safe rename migration.
Computed paths match production behavior on every reference; no migration
machinery is required for projects that have already adopted the encoded
format.

* [[jjdhm_paces]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_map axd_property
{jjdhm_paces}
{jjdt_coronet}
{jjdpr_pace}
is the dictionary of all
{jjdpr_pace_s}
within this
{jjdhr_heat},
keyed by
{jjdt_coronet}.
Serialized as `jjghn_paces` in JSON, with lexicographic key order for deterministic output.
Execution order governed by
{jjdhm_order},
not key order.

==== Status Values

[[jjdhe_racing]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdhe_racing}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is actively being worked.
Serialized as `jjghe_racing` in JSON.

[[jjdhe_stabled]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdhe_stabled}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is paused, not actively worked.
Serialized as `jjghe_stabled` in JSON.

[[jjdhe_retired]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdhe_retired}::

1479	=== {jjdpr_pace}
1480	
1481	[[jjdpr_pace]]
1482	//axl_voices axr_record_json
1483	{jjdpr_pace}::
1484	A discrete action within a
1485	{jjdhr_heat}.
1486	Keyed in JSON by
1487	{jjdt_coronet}.
1488	
1489	==== Members
1490	
1491	* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
1492	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_positional
1493	{jjdpm_coronet}
1494	is the
1495	{jjdpr_pace_p}
1496	globally unique identity.
1497	Type:

1499	Encoded as the JSON map key of this Pace's entry in

1501	no separate property name.

1503	* [[jjdpm_tacks]]
1504	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated axd_property
1505	{jjdpm_tacks}
1506	is the ordered history of state snapshots for this
1507	{jjdpr_pace}.
1508	Element type:
1509	{jjdcr_tack}.
1510	Index 0 is the current (most recent) tack;
1511	{jjsgmpt_prepend_tack}
1512	inserts at position zero.
1513	Serialized as `jjgpn_tacks` in JSON.

1515	==== State Values
1516	
1517	[[jjdpe_rough]]

1528	The
1529	{jjdpr_pace}
1530	has been successfully finished.
1531	Serialized as `jjgte_complete` in JSON.

1543	{jjdpe_bridled}::
1544	V3-legacy state: human-claimed for interactive work.
1545	Serialized as `jjgte_bridled` in JSON (serde alias: `primed`).
1546	Present in code but deprecated.

1548	New operations MUST NOT produce this state.
1549	
1550	==== State Classification Predicates
1551	
1552	Predicates for querying pace states by semantic category.
1553	
1554	[[jjdpe_defined]] {jjdpe_defined}:: Pace contributes to heat's scope.
1555	* rough: true
1556	* complete: true
1557	* abandoned: false
1558	
1559	[[jjdpe_resolved]] {jjdpe_resolved}:: Pace requires no further action.
1560	* rough: false
1561	* complete: true
1562	* abandoned: true
1563	
1564	Future states declare their predicate values when added. Query logic uses predicates, not state names.

1627	=== {jjdyr_table}
1628	
1629	[[jjdyr_table]]
1630	//axl_voices axo_entity
1631	{jjdyr_table}::
1632	A structured column layout for formatted output.
1633	Provides single source of truth for headers, widths, and alignment.
1634	Eliminates magic numbers and strings from output code.
1635	
1636	==== Members
1637	
1638	* [[jjdym_column]]
1639	//axl_voices axr_member
1640	{jjdym_column}
1641	is a column definition with header, width, and alignment.
1642	
1643	* [[jjdym_header]]
1644	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required

1651	{jjdym_align}
1652	specifies the column alignment.
1653	Values:
1654	{jjdye_left}
1655	or
1656	{jjdye_right}.
1657	
1658	==== Alignment Values
1659	
1660	[[jjdye_left]]
1661	//axl_voices axt_enum_value
1662	{jjdye_left}::
1663	Left-align column content.
1664	
1665	[[jjdye_right]]
1666	//axl_voices axt_enum_value
1667	{jjdye_right}::
1668	Right-align column content.
1669	
1670	=== {jjsz_gazette}
1671	
1672	[[jjsz_gazette]]
1673	//axve_entity
1674	{jjsz_gazette}::
1675	A structured container for tagged notices in markdown format, used for multiline data exchange between
1676	{jjdx_tool_s}
1677	and Claude.
1678	Each gazette is constructed with a vocabulary of allowed slugs that constrains its contents.
1679	Notices are keyed by (slug, lede) pairs; the gazette is a two-level map from slug to lede to content.
1680	+
1681	The physical exchange medium is {jjdxo_gazette} within the {jjdxo_exchange}.
1682	Operations write gazette output to this file; Claude reads it.
1683	Claude writes gazette input to this file; operations read it.
1684	+
1685	Construction is either by parsing raw markdown (producing a frozen gazette with diagnostic error reporting)
1686	or by programmatic building (producing an unfrozen gazette that freezes on first content disclosure).
1687	+
1688	Contract elaborated in subdocument:
1689	
1690	include::JJSCGZ-gazette.adoc[leveloffset=+2]
1691	
1692	== Serialization
1693	
1694	[[jjdz_charset]]
1695	{jjdz_charset}::
1696	The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
1697	Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
1698	`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
1699	Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
1700	This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
1701	Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
1702	
1703	[[jjdz_encoding]]
1704	{jjdz_encoding}::
1705	The algorithm for encoding
1706	{jjdt_firemark}
1707	and
1708	{jjdt_coronet}
1709	values.
1710	+
1711	*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
1712	+
1713	. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
1714	. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
1715	. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
1716	+
1717	*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
1718	+
1719	. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
1720	. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
1721	. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
1722	. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
1723	. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
1724	+
1725	*Input flexibility:*
1726	+
1727	CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
1728	Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
1729	Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).
1730

1873	[[jjda_size_limit]]
1874	//axl_voices axa_keyword
1875	{jjda_size_limit}::
1876	Override the byte ceiling enforced by the
1877	{jjdr_persist}
1878	size guard.
1879	Optional integer; default 50KB, uniform across all
1880	{jjdx_tool_s}
1881	that accept this argument.
1882	+
1883	The guard's purpose is byte-sanity review: it surfaces unintended bulk before a commit lands, expecting the operator to confirm the bytes are legitimate and authorize a raise on retry.
1884	Per-operation defaults are deliberately *not* part of this contract — uniform 50KB ensures the review fires on the same threshold everywhere, eliminating drift and the false-confidence failure mode where a silently-higher op-specific ceiling masks bulk that the operator believed was being checked.
1885	+
1886	Accepted by
1887	{jjdx_tool_s}
1888	whose own delta has a variable-size content channel:
1889	{jjdo_record},
1890	{jjdo_close},
1891	{jjdo_archive},
1892	{jjdo_paddock},
1893	{jjdo_enroll},
1894	{jjdo_redocket},
1895	{jjdo_landing},
1896	{jjdo_transfer}.
1897	Structural-mutator
1898	{jjdx_tool_s}
1899	({jjdo_create},
1900	{jjdo_alter},
1901	{jjdo_relabel},
1902	{jjdo_drop},
1903	{jjdo_reorder},
1904	{jjdo_relocate},
1905	{jjdo_continue})
1906	do not accept this argument; their commit deltas are bounded by fixed-shape
1907	{jjdgr_gallops}
1908	mutations.

1966	== Commit Message Architecture
1967	
1968	All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
1969	- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
1970	- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
1971	- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

2002	== Operation Taxonomy
2003	
2004	This section defines the structural vocabulary for how operations decompose into
2005	spec-governed layers.
2006	Each MCP command ({jjsoprd_revise_docket}, etc.) is a *procedure* that delegates to
2007	a *composed method* on {jjdgr_gallops}, which itself composes *shared primitive methods*.
2008	The spec names every layer so implementation cannot drift from design.
2009	
2010	=== Dispatch Protocol
2011	
2012	[[jjsodp_command_lifecycle]]
2013	{jjsodp_command_lifecycle}::
2014	The uniform frame that the MCP dispatcher applies to every {jjdx_tool} call.
2015	Two layers: an {jjdxo_officium} envelope that wraps all commands, and a {jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle for commands that touch persistent state.
2016	+
2017	*{jjdxo_officium} envelope* (all commands except {jjdo_open}):
2018	+
2019	. Validate {jjdxo_officium} parameter — {jjdxo_exchange} directory must exist
2020	. Touch {jjdxo_heartbeat} file in the {jjdxo_exchange} directory
2021	+
2022	{jjdo_open} is exempt: it creates the {jjdxo_officium} rather than validating one.
2023	If the {jjdxo_exchange} directory is missing (stale session, incorrect ID), the dispatcher returns an error prompting the agent to call {jjdo_open}.
2024	+
2025	*{jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle* (gallops-touching commands):
2026	+
2027	Per {jjdk_sole_operator}, every gallops operation acquires the lock unconditionally.
2028	+
2029	{jjds_behavior}
2030	+
2031	. Acquire commit lock
2032	. {jjdr_load} — deserialize {jjdgr_gallops} from JSON
2033	. Call the procedure handler with `&mut` {jjdgr_gallops} and deserialized parameters
2034	. {jjdr_persist} — save {jjdgr_gallops} and commit with affiliated firemark
2035	. Return the handler's output text as MCP response
2036	+
2037	The lifecycle is implemented once in the MCP dispatcher.
2038	Procedures never acquire locks, load {jjdgr_gallops}, or call {jjdr_persist} directly.
2039	The handler returns output text on success or an error — it has no knowledge of
2040	locks, persistence, or commit messages.
2041	+
2042	The dispatcher determines the affiliated firemark from the command's parameters.
2043	Heat-affiliated commands supply a {jjdt_firemark} directly.
2044	Pace-affiliated commands supply a {jjdt_coronet}; the dispatcher derives the
2045	parent {jjdt_firemark}.
2046	+
2047	{jjdo_open} bypasses the {jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle entirely — it is infrastructure that does not touch persistent state.
2048	+
2049	*{jjsz_gazette} I/O wiring* (output and input operations):
2050	+
2051	Output operations ({jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_show}, {jjdo_paddock} getter):
2052	
2053	. Dispatcher creates an empty {jjsz_gazette} with appropriate vocabulary
2054	. Dispatcher passes `&mut` gazette to the handler
2055	. Handler calls `jjrz_add` on the gazette to populate notices
2056	. After the handler returns, dispatcher emits the gazette to {jjdxo_gazette} (skips write if gazette is empty)
2057	+
2058	Input operations ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjsoprd_revise_docket}, {jjdo_paddock} setter):
2059	
2060	. Dispatcher reads {jjdxo_gazette} at the start of dispatch
2061	. If gazette file is non-empty, dispatcher immediately overwrites it with empty content (consume)
2062	. Non-empty content is parsed via the appropriate gazette parse function; parsed values replace inline params
2063	. If gazette file is empty, dispatcher falls back to inline params from the MCP call
2064	. Both paths converge before calling the handler — the handler receives the resolved values regardless of source
2065

2074	=== Procedures
2075	
2076	[[jjsoprd_revise_docket]]
2077	//axvo_procedure axd_transient axd_grouped
2078	{jjsoprd_revise_docket}::
2079	Update a {jjdpr_pace_p} docket text, member of {jjsogmc_mcp_commands}.
2080	Runs within {jjsodp_command_lifecycle} (pace-affiliated).
2081	+
2082	{jjds_arguments}
2083	+
2084	* {jjdt_coronet} — target {jjdpr_pace}.
2085	  Required when {jjdxo_gazette} is empty; parsed from gazette lede when gazette is present.
2086	* `docket` (string) — new docket text.
2087	  Required when {jjdxo_gazette} is empty; parsed from gazette content when gazette is present.
2088	  When gazette is present, multiple (coronet, docket) pairs may be provided as separate gazette notices.
2089	+
2090	{jjds_behavior}
2091	+
2092	. Capture basis SHA and timestamp at I/O boundary
2093	. Call {jjsgmrd_revise_docket} with coronet, docket, basis, and timestamp
2094	. Return output text
2095	+
2096	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
2097	
2098	=== Gallops Methods
2099	
2100	[[jjsgmrp_resolve_pace]]
2101	//axvo_method axd_transient
2102	{jjsgmrp_resolve_pace}::
2103	Navigate {jjdgr_gallops} from a {jjdt_coronet} to the target {jjdpr_pace} and its current tack state.
2104	Returns pace context: parsed coronet, firemark key, current state, text, silks, and direction.
2105	Used by all pace-targeting composed methods as the shared read primitive.
2106	
2107	[[jjsgmpt_prepend_tack]]
2108	//axvo_method axd_transient
2109	{jjsgmpt_prepend_tack}::
2110	Insert a new tack at position zero of a {jjdpr_pace_p} tack history within {jjdgr_gallops}.
2111	Used by all pace-mutating composed methods as the shared write primitive.
2112	
2113	[[jjsgmrd_revise_docket]]
2114	//axvo_method axd_transient
2115	{jjsgmrd_revise_docket}::
2116	Pure state transform on {jjdgr_gallops} that updates a {jjdpr_pace_p} docket text.
2117	Takes caller-provided basis SHA and timestamp (I/O captured at procedure boundary).
2118	+
2119	{jjds_behavior}
2120	+
2121	. {jjsgmrp_resolve_pace} — obtain current pace context
2122	. Create new tack with updated text, inherited state and silks, caller-provided basis and timestamp
2123	. {jjsgmpt_prepend_tack} — commit to pace history
2124	+
2125	Returns pace context for the calling procedure.

2271	=== Officium Operations
2272	
2273	[[jjdo_open]]
2274	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2275	==== {jjdo_open}
2276	
2277	Create a new {jjdxo_officium} for the calling agent session.
2278	Takes no parameters; returns the {jjdxo_officium} identity string.
2279	Does not touch {jjdgr_gallops}.
2280	+
2281	{jjds_behavior}
2282	+
2283	. Execute {jjdxo_invitatory} procedure ({jjdxo_exsanguination} → identity → directory → probe → commit)
2284	. Return {jjdxo_exsanguination} summary and identity string
2285	+
2286	The agent calls {jjdo_open} once per chat session, at the beginning.
2287	The returned identity becomes a required parameter on all subsequent {jjdx_tool} calls.
2288	+
2289	{jjds_stdout}
2290	+
2291	When {jjdxo_exsanguination} encounters prior officia, a summary line: `Exsanguination: N active, M reaped`.
2292	Then the {jjdxo_officium} identity string, prefixed with ☉.
2293	
2294	=== Remote Dispatch Operations
2295	
2296	Operations for executing commands and managing dispatches on a
2297	{jjdxr_fundus}.
2298	These are
2299	{jjdxo_officium}
2300	infrastructure operations — they bypass the
2301	{jjdgr_gallops}
2302	lifecycle entirely.
2303	{jjdo_relay}
2304	increments
2305	{jjdhm_pensum_seed}
2306	in officium-local storage.
2307	Member of
2308	{jjsogrd_remote_dispatch}.
2309	
2310	[[jjsogrd_remote_dispatch]]
2311	//axvo_group

2313	The collection of remote dispatch operations:
2314	{jjdo_bind},

2321	[[jjdo_bind]]
2322	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2323	==== {jjdo_bind}
2324	
2325	//axhob_operation
2326	{jjdo_bind}
2327	
2328	Create a
2329	{jjdt_legatio}
2330	session handle by probing the
2331	{jjdxr_fundus}
2332	via SSH and validating
2333	{jjdt_reldir}
2334	per
2335	{jjdk_reldir_safety}.
2336	
2337	{jjds_arguments}
2338	
2339	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2340	{jjda_alias}

2343	— {xref_BURN} alias identifying the target connection.
2344	Resolved on the
2345	{jjdxr_curia}
2346	to extract `BURN_HOST`, `BURN_USER`, and `BURN_COMMAND` (which may be empty for platforms using the default shell).
2347	
2348	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2349	{jjdt_reldir}
2350	//axd_required

2352	— relative path from user's home to the project directory on the
2353	{jjdxr_fundus}
2354	
2355	//axhoot_typed_output
2356	{jjdt_legatio}
2357	{jjds_stdout}
2358	{jjdt_legatio}
2359	token and probe results
2360	
2361	//axhoc_completion
2362	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
2363	
2364	{jjds_behavior}
2365	
2366	//axhos_step
2367	. Resolve {xref_BURN} on the
2368	{jjdxr_curia}:
2369	kindle `{alias}/burn.env` from the station user's BURN directory, extract `BURN_HOST`, `BURN_USER`, `BURN_ALIAS`, and `BURN_COMMAND`.
2370	`BURN_COMMAND` may be empty — platforms using the default shell (Linux, macOS) omit it.
2371	//axhos_step
2372	. Validate
2373	{jjdt_reldir}
2374	(Layer 1 —
2375	{jjdk_reldir_safety}):
2376	must not be empty, must not start with `/` or `.`, must contain at least one `/`
2377	//axhos_step
2378	. SSH probe (Layer 3 —
2379	{jjdk_reldir_safety}):
2380	connect via `BURN_ALIAS` (which routes through `~/.ssh/config` to the correct host, user, and key),
2381	execute remote shell script on
2382	{jjdxr_fundus}
2383	that resolves `$HOME/{reldir}` to an absolute path, verifies it is strictly under `$HOME`, and verifies minimum depth (at least 2 components under `$HOME`).
2384	Expect `PROBE_OK` sentinel on success.
2385	//axhos_step
2386	. Read `$HOME/{reldir}/.buk/burc.env` from
2387	{jjdxr_fundus}
2388	via SSH (using `BURN_ALIAS`) and extract `BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR`
2389	//axhos_step
2390	. Mint
2391	{jjdt_legatio}
2392	token: scan existing legatio files in
2393	{jjdxo_officium}
2394	directory, assign next sequential `L{N}` token
2395	//axhos_step
2396	. Persist legatio state (alias, reldir, host, user [resolved from BURN], output_root_dir) as JSON in
2397	{jjdxo_officium}
2398	directory
2399	//axhos_step
2400	. Output
2401	{jjdt_legatio}
2402	token and binding summary
2403	
2404	[[jjdo_send]]
2405	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2406	==== {jjdo_send}
2407	
2408	//axhob_operation
2409	{jjdo_send}
2410	
2411	Synchronous remote execution via
2412	{jjdt_legatio}.
2413	Single command string interpreted by `bash -c` on the
2414	{jjdxr_fundus}
2415	after `cd RELDIR`.
2416	
2417	{jjds_arguments}
2418	
2419	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2420	{jjdt_legatio}
2421	//axd_required
2422	* {jjda_legatio}
2423	—
2424	{jjdt_legatio}
2425	token from
2426	{jjdo_bind}
2427	
2428	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2429	{jjda_command}
2430	//axd_required
2431	* {jjda_command}
2432	— shell command string to execute on the
2433	{jjdxr_fundus}
2434	
2435	//axhoc_completion
2436	{jjds_stdout} Remote stdout, stderr, and exit code
2437	
2438	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 on SSH transport success, non-zero on SSH transport failure.
2439	
2440	{jjds_behavior}
2441	
2442	//axhos_step
2443	. Load
2444	{jjdt_legatio}
2445	state from
2446	{jjdxo_officium}
2447	directory
2448	//axhos_step
2449	. Construct remote command: `cd "$HOME/{reldir}" && bash -c {quoted_command}`
2450	(POSIX single-quote wrapping with `'\''` for embedded quotes)
2451	//axhos_step
2452	. Execute via SSH with `BatchMode=yes` and `ConnectTimeout=10`
2453	//axhos_step
2454	. Capture remote stdout, stderr, and exit code
2455	//axhos_step
2456	. Output remote stdout, stderr, and `Exit: {code}`
2457	
2458	[[jjdo_relay]]
2459	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2460	==== {jjdo_relay}
2461	
2462	//axhob_operation
2463	{jjdo_relay}
2464	
2465	Asynchronous dispatch via nohup on the
2466	{jjdxr_fundus}.
2467	Mints a
2468	{jjdt_pensum}
2469	token (incrementing
2470	{jjdhm_pensum_seed}
2471	in officium-local storage — no
2472	{jjdgr_gallops}
2473	lock or commit), sets `BURE_LABEL`, launches via SSH, captures `burx.env` start fields via a second SSH connection.
2474	
2475	{jjds_arguments}
2476	
2477	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2478	{jjdt_legatio}
2479	//axd_required
2480	* {jjda_legatio}
2481	—
2482	{jjdt_legatio}
2483	token from
2484	{jjdo_bind}
2485	
2486	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2487	{jjda_tabtarget}
2488	//axd_required
2489	* {jjda_tabtarget}
2490	— bare filename of the tabtarget to dispatch (e.g., `rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh`).
2491	Must end with `.sh`, no path components.
2492	
2493	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2494	{jjda_timeout}
2495	//axd_required
2496	* {jjda_timeout}
2497	— maximum execution time in seconds.
2498	A watchdog on the
2499	{jjdxr_fundus}
2500	kills the tabtarget after this duration.
2501	
2502	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2503	{jjdt_firemark}
2504	//axd_required
2505	* {jjdt_firemark}
2506	— parent
2507	{jjdhr_heat}
2508	identity (used for
2509	{jjdt_pensum}
2510	token minting under
2511	{jjdgr_gallops})
2512	
2513	//axhoot_typed_output
2514	{jjdt_pensum}
2515	{jjds_stdout}
2516	{jjdt_pensum}
2517	token and remote temp directory path
2518	
2519	//axhoc_completion
2520	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
2521	
2522	{jjds_behavior}
2523	
2524	//axhos_step
2525	. Verify {jjdxr_curia} readiness:
2526	.. Working tree must be clean (`git status --porcelain` empty). If dirty, fail with file count.
2527	.. HEAD must equal `origin/<branch>` (`git rev-parse` comparison). If unpushed, fail with abbreviated SHAs.
2528	//axhos_step
2529	. Validate tabtarget: must not be empty, must not contain `/` or `..`, must end with `.sh`
2530	//axhos_step
2531	. Load
2532	{jjdt_legatio}
2533	state from
2534	{jjdxo_officium}
2535	directory
2536	//axhos_step
2537	. Mint
2538	{jjdt_pensum}
2539	token:
2540	.. Read `pensum_seeds.json` from
2541	{jjdxo_officium}
2542	directory
2543	.. Increment
2544	{jjdhm_pensum_seed}
2545	for this
2546	{jjdt_firemark}
2547	.. Write `pensum_seeds.json` back (no commit lock, no git commit)
2548	//axhos_step
2549	. SSH #1 — launch nohup wrapper on
2550	{jjdxr_fundus}:
2551	.. Set `BURE_LABEL` to
2552	{jjdt_pensum}
2553	token in environment
2554	.. Background the tabtarget via nohup with timeout watchdog
2555	.. Expect `RELAY_OK` sentinel on successful launch
2556	//axhos_step
2557	. SSH #2 — poll for initial `burx.env`:
2558	.. Read `{output_root_dir}/current/burx.env` from
2559	{jjdxr_fundus}
2560	(up to 10 retries, 1 second apart)
2561	.. Validate `BURX_LABEL` matches
2562	{jjdt_pensum}
2563	token
2564	.. Extract `BURX_TEMP_DIR` and `BURX_PID`
2565	//axhos_step
2566	. Persist
2567	{jjdxp_pensum}
2568	state (legatio, firemark, temp_dir, pid, tabtarget, timeout, began_at) as JSON in
2569	{jjdxo_officium}
2570	directory
2571	//axhos_step
2572	. Output
2573	{jjdt_pensum}
2574	token and remote temp directory
2575	
2576	[[jjdo_check]]
2577	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2578	==== {jjdo_check}
2579	
2580	//axhob_operation
2581	{jjdo_check}
2582	
2583	Probe or poll a
2584	{jjdxp_pensum_p}
2585	status on the
2586	{jjdxr_fundus}.
2587	Required timeout parameter: 0 = instant probe, >0 = poll until terminal or expiry.
2588	Returns BURX fields, liveness report
2589	({jjdxpe_running},
2590	{jjdxpe_orphaned},
2591	{jjdxpe_stopped},
2592	or
2593	{jjdxpe_lost}),
2594	and temp dir file list on terminal status.
2595	
2596	{jjds_arguments}
2597	
2598	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2599	{jjdt_pensum}
2600	//axd_required
2601	* {jjda_pensum}
2602	—
2603	{jjdt_pensum}
2604	token from
2605	{jjdo_relay}
2606	
2607	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2608	{jjda_timeout}
2609	//axd_required
2610	* {jjda_timeout}
2611	— probe timeout in seconds.
2612	0 = instant single probe.
2613	>0 = poll until terminal state or timeout expiry (2-second intervals).
2614	
2615	//axhoc_completion
2616	{jjds_stdout} BURX fields, liveness report, file list (on terminal status)
2617	
2618	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 on successful probe (regardless of
2619	{jjdxp_pensum}
2620	status), non-zero on probe failure.
2621	
2622	{jjds_behavior}
2623	
2624	//axhos_step
2625	. Parse and validate
2626	{jjdt_pensum}
2627	token
2628	//axhos_step
2629	. Load
2630	{jjdxp_pensum}
2631	state from
2632	{jjdxo_officium}
2633	directory
2634	//axhos_step
2635	. Load
2636	{jjdt_legatio}
2637	state referenced by
2638	{jjdxp_pensum}
2639	//axhos_step
2640	. Enter probe loop:
2641	.. If timeout = 0: single probe, exit loop after first result
2642	.. If timeout > 0: poll until terminal state or deadline (2-second intervals)
2643	//axhos_step
2644	. Each probe executes SSH script on
2645	{jjdxr_fundus}:
2646	.. If `burx.env` file missing: report
2647	{jjdxpe_lost}
2648	.. If `BURX_EXIT_STATUS` present in `burx.env`: report
2649	{jjdxpe_stopped}
2650	and list files in temp directory
2651	.. If PID alive (`kill -0`): report
2652	{jjdxpe_running}
2653	.. If PID dead and no exit status: report
2654	{jjdxpe_orphaned}
2655	//axhos_step
2656	. Output BURX fields (ordered): `BURX_PID`, `BURX_BEGAN_AT`, `BURX_TABTARGET`, `BURX_TEMP_DIR`, `BURX_TRANSCRIPT`, `BURX_LOG_HIST`, `BURX_LABEL`, `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`, `BURX_ENDED_AT`
2657	//axhos_step
2658	. On terminal status
2659	({jjdxpe_stopped}),
2660	output file list from `BURX_TEMP_DIR`
2661	
2662	[[jjdo_fetch]]
2663	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2664	==== {jjdo_fetch}
2665	
2666	//axhob_operation
2667	{jjdo_fetch}
2668	
2669	Single file read from
2670	{jjdxr_fundus}
2671	via
2672	{jjdt_legatio}.
2673	Path relative to RELDIR or absolute.
2674	
2675	{jjds_arguments}
2676	
2677	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2678	{jjdt_legatio}
2679	//axd_required
2680	* {jjda_legatio}
2681	—
2682	{jjdt_legatio}
2683	token from
2684	{jjdo_bind}
2685	
2686	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2687	{jjda_path}
2688	//axd_required
2689	* {jjda_path}
2690	— file path on the
2691	{jjdxr_fundus}.
2692	If starts with `/`: absolute path.
2693	Otherwise: relative to `$HOME/{reldir}`.
2694	
2695	//axhoc_completion
2696	{jjds_stdout} File contents (UTF-8 lossy)
2697	
2698	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
2699	
2700	{jjds_behavior}
2701	
2702	//axhos_step
2703	. Load
2704	{jjdt_legatio}
2705	state from
2706	{jjdxo_officium}
2707	directory
2708	//axhos_step
2709	. Resolve path:
2710	.. If path starts with `/`: use as absolute
2711	.. Otherwise: relative to `$HOME/{reldir}`
2712	//axhos_step
2713	. Execute SSH `cat` of the resolved path on the
2714	{jjdxr_fundus}
2715	//axhos_step
2716	. Output file contents to stdout

2718	[[jjdo_plant]]
2719	//axvo_procedure axd_transient
2720	==== {jjdo_plant}
2721	
2722	//axhob_operation
2723	{jjdo_plant}
2724	
2725	Reset
2726	{jjdxr_fundus}
2727	workspace to an exact git commit.
2728	Synchronous over SSH, fail-fast.
2729	{jjdt_reldir}
2730	safety is not re-checked —
2731	{jjdo_bind}
2732	already validated it.
2733	
2734	{jjds_arguments}
2735	
2736	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2737	{jjdt_legatio}
2738	//axd_required
2739	* {jjda_legatio}
2740	—
2741	{jjdt_legatio}
2742	token from
2743	{jjdo_bind}
2744	
2745	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2746	{jjda_commit}
2747	//axd_required
2748	* {jjda_commit}
2749	— git ref (SHA, branch, tag) to reset the
2750	{jjdxr_fundus}
2751	workspace to.
2752	Must contain only safe characters (alphanumeric, `/`, `-`, `_`, `.`).
2753	
2754	//axhoc_completion
2755	{jjds_stdout} Final HEAD SHA on success
2756	
2757	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
2758	
2759	{jjds_behavior}
2760	
2761	//axhos_step
2762	. Validate git ref: must not be empty, must contain only safe characters
2763	//axhos_step
2764	. Load
2765	{jjdt_legatio}
2766	state from
2767	{jjdxo_officium}
2768	directory
2769	//axhos_step
2770	. Execute SSH script on
2771	{jjdxr_fundus}:
2772	.. `cd "$HOME/{reldir}"` or exit
2773	.. `git fetch origin` or exit
2774	.. `git reset --hard {commit}` or exit
2775	.. `git clean -dxf` or exit
2776	.. Output `PLANT_OK:{HEAD_SHA}` sentinel
2777	//axhos_step
2778	. Parse sentinel to extract final HEAD
2779	//axhos_step
2780	. Output planting result
2781	
2782	=== Steeplechase Operations
2783	
2784	The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
2785	{jjdgr_gallops}
2786	JSON.
2787	Commit messages follow patterns that
2788	{jjdo_log}
2789	can parse to reconstruct session history.
2790	
2791	==== Commit Message Patterns
2792	
2793	All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
2794	
2795	* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
2796	* `BRAND` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max brand from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
2797	* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
2798	* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
2799	
2800	*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_record}):
2801	----
2802	jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
2803	
2804	Co-Authored-By: ...
2805	----
2806	
2807	Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
2808	
2809	*Landing marker* (created when spawned agent completes):
2810	----
2811	jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:L: {agent} {landed|crashed}
2812	
2813	{agent completion report}
2814	
2815	Co-Authored-By: ...
2816	----
2817	
2818	Subject format: `{agent} {landed|crashed}` where agent is the executor tier (haiku/sonnet/opus) and landed/crashed indicates success/failure. Body contains the agent's raw completion report.
2819	
2820	*{jjdxo_invitatory} marker* (created by {jjdo_open}):
2821	----
2822	jjb:BRAND::i: OFFICIUM <id>
2823	
2824	session: 1649a554-e779-454e-8490-58f40d55c04d
2825	cwd: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster
2826	haiku: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
2827	sonnet: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
2828	opus: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
2829	host: macbook-pro.local
2830	platform: darwin-arm64
2831	----
2832	+
2833	The `session:` and `cwd:` lines appear on every {jjdxo_invitatory}.
2834	The model/host/platform inventory below them appears only on the first {jjdxo_invitatory} of the day; subsequent same-day invitatories carry `session:` + `cwd:` alone.
2835	See <<jjdxo_invitatory>> for the recall workflow (`git log --grep='session: …'` + `claude --resume`).
2836	
2837	{jjdxo_invitatory} markers record when an {jjdxo_officium} opens.
2838	Created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}) — not by {jjdx_mcp} process startup.
2839	Model IDs are collected via `vvcp_probe()`, which spawns parallel claude invocations via tokio async subprocess spawning.
2840	+
2841	The {jjdxo_compline} action code `o` is reserved-unused. No compline commits are created.
2842	
2843	Pace-level action codes:
2844	
2845	* `n` — Notch: standard commit
2846	* `L` — LANDING: records agent return after autonomous execution
2847	* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
2848	* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
2849	
2850	*Heat-level operations*:
2851	----
2852	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:N: heat-silks
2853	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:S: pace-silks
2854	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:s: YYMMDD-HHMM session  (superseded — see below)
2855	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:r: reordered
2856	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
2857	jjb:BRAND:₣XX:R: heat-silks
2858	----
2859	
2860	Heat-level action codes:
2861	
2862	* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
2863	* `S` — Slate: add new pace
2864	* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
2865	* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
2866	* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
2867	
2868	NOTE: {jjdxo_invitatory} commits use action code `i`.
2869	The {jjdxo_compline} action code `o` is reserved-unused — sessions end without ceremony.
2870	See <<jjdxo_invitatory>> and <<jjdxo_compline>> for design rationale.
2871

2893	
2894	== V3 Legacy Schema Reference
2895	
2896	WARNING: This section is a frozen reference for V3→V4 migration.
2897	It documents the V3 schema as implemented in `jjrt_v3_types.rs`.
2898	Do NOT modify — will be removed by ₣An (jjk-v4-release-and-legacy-removal).
2899	
2900	=== V3 Pace Record
2901	
2902	In V3, a {jjdpr_pace} contains a `tacks` array of Tack records.
2903	Each Tack captures a snapshot of the pace's state at a point in time.
2904	
2905	==== V3 Tack Record
2906	
2907	* `ts` (string, required) — timestamp
2908	* `state` (enum, required) — pace state (see V3 Pace State Enum)
2909	* `text` (string, required) — docket text at this snapshot
2910	* `silks` (string, required) — pace silks at this snapshot
2911	* `commit` (string, required) — basis commit SHA (Rust field: `basis`, serde rename: `commit`)
2912	* `direction` (string, optional) — plan/direction text
2913	
2914	==== V3 Pace State Enum
2915	
2916	* `rough` — needs clarification
2917	* `bridled` — human-claimed for interactive work (serde alias: `primed`)
2918	* `complete` — finished
2919	* `abandoned` — stopped without completion
2920	
2921	=== V3 Heat Record
2922	
2923	Same as V4 (no structural changes to Heat in V4 migration).
2924	
2925	=== V3 Gallops Record
2926	
2927	Same as V4 except:
2928	
2929	* `schema_version` absent (added in V4)
2930	* `heat_order` absent (added in V4; populated from sorted BTreeMap keys on migration)
2931	
2932	=== V3→V4 Migration Path
2933	
2934	Migration is handled by {jjdr_load} in `jjri_io.rs`.
2935	Current behavior:
2936	
2937	. Deserialize JSON as current types
2938	. Detect migration mode: `schema_version` absent or `heat_order` empty
2939	. Set `schema_version` to 4
2940	. Populate `heat_order` from sorted BTreeMap keys
2941	. Next {jjdr_save} writes V4 format
2942	
2943	The frozen V3 types in `jjrt_v3_types.rs` capture the V3 schema for reference when V4 type changes are implemented.
2944	
2945

file.content:
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is complete and archived.
Serialized as `jjghe_retired` in JSON.

=== {jjdpr_pace}

[[jjdpr_pace]]
//axl_voices axr_record_json
{jjdpr_pace}::
A discrete action within a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_coronet}.

==== Members

* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_positional
{jjdpm_coronet}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
globally unique identity.
Type:
{jjdt_coronet}.
Encoded as the JSON map key of this Pace's entry in
{jjdhm_paces};
no separate property name.

* [[jjdpm_tacks]]
//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated axd_property
{jjdpm_tacks}
is the ordered history of state snapshots for this
{jjdpr_pace}.
Element type:
{jjdcr_tack}.
Index 0 is the current (most recent) tack;
{jjsgmpt_prepend_tack}
inserts at position zero.
Serialized as `jjgpn_tacks` in JSON.

==== State Values

[[jjdpe_rough]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdpe_rough}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
needs clarification before work can proceed.
Serialized as `jjgte_rough` in JSON.

[[jjdpe_complete]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdpe_complete}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
has been successfully finished.
Serialized as `jjgte_complete` in JSON.

[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string

==== State Classification Predicates

Predicates for querying pace states by semantic category.

[[jjdpe_defined]] {jjdpe_defined}:: Pace contributes to heat's scope.
* rough: true
* complete: true
* abandoned: false

[[jjdpe_resolved]] {jjdpe_resolved}:: Pace requires no further action.
* rough: false
* complete: true
* abandoned: true

Future states declare their predicate values when added. Query logic uses predicates, not state names.

=== {jjsz_gazette}

[[jjsz_gazette]]
//axve_entity
{jjsz_gazette}::
A structured container for tagged notices in markdown format, used for multiline data exchange between
{jjdx_tool_s}
and Claude.
Each gazette is constructed with a vocabulary of allowed slugs that constrains its contents.
Notices are keyed by (slug, lede) pairs; the gazette is a two-level map from slug to lede to content.
+
The physical exchange medium is {jjdxo_gazette} within the {jjdxo_exchange}.
Operations write gazette output to this file; Claude reads it.
Claude writes gazette input to this file; operations read it.
+
Construction is either by parsing raw markdown (producing a frozen gazette with diagnostic error reporting)
or by programmatic building (producing an unfrozen gazette that freezes on first content disclosure).
+
Contract elaborated in subdocument:

include::JJSCGZ-gazette.adoc[leveloffset=+2]

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

[[jjda_size_limit]]
//axl_voices axa_keyword
{jjda_size_limit}::
Override the byte ceiling enforced by the
{jjdr_persist}
size guard.
Optional integer; default 50KB, uniform across all
{jjdx_tool_s}
that accept this argument.
+
The guard's purpose is byte-sanity review: it surfaces unintended bulk before a commit lands, expecting the operator to confirm the bytes are legitimate and authorize a raise on retry.
Per-operation defaults are deliberately *not* part of this contract — uniform 50KB ensures the review fires on the same threshold everywhere, eliminating drift and the false-confidence failure mode where a silently-higher op-specific ceiling masks bulk that the operator believed was being checked.
+
Accepted by
{jjdx_tool_s}
whose own delta has a variable-size content channel:
{jjdo_record},
{jjdo_close},
{jjdo_archive},
{jjdo_paddock},
{jjdo_enroll},
{jjdo_redocket},
{jjdo_landing},
{jjdo_transfer}.
Structural-mutator
{jjdx_tool_s}
({jjdo_create},
{jjdo_alter},
{jjdo_relabel},
{jjdo_drop},
{jjdo_reorder},
{jjdo_relocate},
{jjdo_continue})
do not accept this argument; their commit deltas are bounded by fixed-shape
{jjdgr_gallops}
mutations.

{jjda_direction}::
V3-legacy.
Plan or warrant text for {jjdpe_bridled} state.
Not exposed as an {jjdx_tool} parameter.
Removal deferred to ₣An.

== Commit Message Architecture

All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

== Operation Taxonomy

This section defines the structural vocabulary for how operations decompose into
spec-governed layers.
Each MCP command ({jjsoprd_revise_docket}, etc.) is a *procedure* that delegates to
a *composed method* on {jjdgr_gallops}, which itself composes *shared primitive methods*.
The spec names every layer so implementation cannot drift from design.

=== Dispatch Protocol

[[jjsodp_command_lifecycle]]
{jjsodp_command_lifecycle}::
The uniform frame that the MCP dispatcher applies to every {jjdx_tool} call.
Two layers: an {jjdxo_officium} envelope that wraps all commands, and a {jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle for commands that touch persistent state.
+
*{jjdxo_officium} envelope* (all commands except {jjdo_open}):
+
. Validate {jjdxo_officium} parameter — {jjdxo_exchange} directory must exist
. Touch {jjdxo_heartbeat} file in the {jjdxo_exchange} directory
+
{jjdo_open} is exempt: it creates the {jjdxo_officium} rather than validating one.
If the {jjdxo_exchange} directory is missing (stale session, incorrect ID), the dispatcher returns an error prompting the agent to call {jjdo_open}.
+
*{jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle* (gallops-touching commands):
+
Per {jjdk_sole_operator}, every gallops operation acquires the lock unconditionally.
+
{jjds_behavior}
+
. Acquire commit lock
. {jjdr_load} — deserialize {jjdgr_gallops} from JSON
. Call the procedure handler with `&mut` {jjdgr_gallops} and deserialized parameters
. {jjdr_persist} — save {jjdgr_gallops} and commit with affiliated firemark
. Return the handler's output text as MCP response
+
The lifecycle is implemented once in the MCP dispatcher.
Procedures never acquire locks, load {jjdgr_gallops}, or call {jjdr_persist} directly.
The handler returns output text on success or an error — it has no knowledge of
locks, persistence, or commit messages.
+
The dispatcher determines the affiliated firemark from the command's parameters.
Heat-affiliated commands supply a {jjdt_firemark} directly.
Pace-affiliated commands supply a {jjdt_coronet}; the dispatcher derives the
parent {jjdt_firemark}.
+
{jjdo_open} bypasses the {jjdgr_gallops} lifecycle entirely — it is infrastructure that does not touch persistent state.
+
*{jjsz_gazette} I/O wiring* (output and input operations):
+
Output operations ({jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_show}, {jjdo_paddock} getter):

. Dispatcher creates an empty {jjsz_gazette} with appropriate vocabulary
. Dispatcher passes `&mut` gazette to the handler
. Handler calls `jjrz_add` on the gazette to populate notices
. After the handler returns, dispatcher emits the gazette to {jjdxo_gazette} (skips write if gazette is empty)
+
Input operations ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjsoprd_revise_docket}, {jjdo_paddock} setter):

. Dispatcher reads {jjdxo_gazette} at the start of dispatch
. If gazette file is non-empty, dispatcher immediately overwrites it with empty content (consume)
. Non-empty content is parsed via the appropriate gazette parse function; parsed values replace inline params
. If gazette file is empty, dispatcher falls back to inline params from the MCP call
. Both paths converge before calling the handler — the handler receives the resolved values regardless of source

=== Groups

[[jjsogmc_mcp_commands]]
//axvo_group
{jjsogmc_mcp_commands}::
The collection of user-visible operations exposed via the {jjdx_cli} MCP tool dispatcher,
